Description: This elegant watch is inspired by the bird island Runde, located on the edge of the sea in Herøy, off Ålesund. Description: The Réti Mark II is a tribute to the iconic chess game between Richard Réti and Savielly Tartakower, played in 1910. This game is one of the most admired in chess history – decided in just 11 moves. Not through a dramatic attack, but with strategic elegance and ice-cold precision. These 11 moves also serve as the watch’s hour markers, and the moves of both players are engraved on the case back as a lasting reminder of Réti’s masterful play.The Réti Mark II is produced in a limited edition of 150 individually numbered pieces per color variant, available with either a blue or green dial.This ...

pages.title: Worden Via Negativa
pages.content:
Product: Worden Via Negativa
Brand/Vendor: WORDEN
Type: Watches
Price: $900.00 CAD
URL: https://watchdna.com/products/007-via-negativa-7-25
Tags: Premium Brands
Description: Limited run of just 25 pieces — with several already sold. "Via Negativa" is a Latin term that translates to "the way of negation." In philosophy and theology, it refers to a method of approaching truth or understanding by defining what something is not rather than by affirming what it is. In design, via negativa is essentially the study of what not to do, and this methodology was used extensively in the development of Worden Watch. This approach can be applied to any creative process and will resonate with artists, free thinkers, and designers alike.
Features:
Styles: DESIGN

Description: Limited to 200 pieces annually: In 2019, MICROMILSPEC launched a test program by distributing 30 timepieces to military personnel within the Norwegian Armed Forces. The goal: to rigorously test the watches in the field and report back on performance and accuracy. These watches were purposefully designed and stripped of all decorations from the bezel, crown, and caseback. A deliberate choice allowing users to easily trace and document their watch’s lifelines. Years later, MICROMILSPEC has remade the watch from the original test program, using design principles and experiences from delivering...

Description: A minimalistic multifunction with a sleek and modern feel, the BOSS Tyler reflects the focus and determination of every BOSS. Angled, brushed lugs add an architectural feel to the watch, which is fitted with a choice of mesh bracelet, link bracelet, or leather strap. Framed by a slim polished bezel, the super-matte dial is detailed with stepped-down day and date sub-eyes, while a range of color combinations ensure the versatility of this modern timepiece.

Description: Bold BOSS style defines the aesthetic of the BOSS Troper, a chronograph with date function fitted with a topstitched leather strap or a refined freefall bracelet with two tapered links. Tags: 
Description: Large Roman numerals contrast with a modern textured dial in the refined BOSS Principle. This three-hand timepiece with date function features domed mineral glass over the watch face for a classic feel, while a stamped pattern at the center of the dial is balanced by a circular brushed outer ring. Designed with a stainless-steel case, the Principle is fitted with a choice of five-link bracelet, mesh bracelet, or crocodile-textured leather strap, to complement a variety of looks.

Description: For the first time, Mido has created a skeleton Swiss watch especially dedicated to women. The new and audacious Rainflower Blossom features delicate lines, directly inspired by the lotus flower shape of Singapore’s contemporary ArtScience Museum. Resolutely feminine petals are interlaced along the edges. Adorned with an openwork flower, the captivating dial reveals at its centre the Caliber 80 – the latest-generation automatic movement, whose mainplate has been specially skeletonised and decorated. It invites the wearer to marvel at the passing of time with audacityand elegance.

It is with this same ambition that Mido continues to equip the Swiss watch Multifort Powerwind with a high technology that allows to offer timekeeping of unparalleled reliability and precision, as in the past.

Tags: 
Description: The Réti Mark II is a tribute to the iconic chess game between Richard Réti and Savielly Tartakower, played in 1910. This game is one of the most admired in chess history – decided in just 11 moves. Not through a dramatic attack, but with strategic elegance and ice-cold precision. These 11 moves also serve as the watch’s hour markers, and the moves of both players are engraved on the case back as a lasting reminder of Réti’s masterful play.The Réti Mark II is produced in a limited edition of 150 individually numbered pieces per color variant, available with either a blue or green dial.This ...

Description: Original aesthetics for an offbeat look. The model is available here with wood and cowhide. It is the ash tree from the vicinity of Praz-sur-Arly that is highlighted for the dial. This tree has an exceptional quality of resistance that is used in the construction of alpine chalets, the very symbol of the mountain. It is cut, sawn and worked on site. These boards, when heated, take on this beautiful caramel-brown color. For a more classic style, a mahogany Ingrassata cowhide leather strap is in harmony.

Tags: 
Description: Bold BOSS style defines the aesthetic of the BOSS Troper, a chronograph with date function fitted with a topstitched leather strap or a refined freefall bracelet with two tapered links. This distinctive-yet-timeless design features a bezel slightly larger than the 44mm case, crown protection, and a double-layer sunray dial with diamond-cut sub-eyes for extra dimension. Easy to wear every day, the Troper is offered in a variety of color combinations.
Features:
Case Size: 44mm
Movement: Chronograph

Tags: 
Description: Large Roman numerals contrast with a modern textured dial in the refined BOSS Principle. This three-hand timepiece with date function features domed mineral glass over the watch face for a classic feel, while a stamped pattern at the center of the dial is balanced by a circular brushed outer ring. Designed with a stainless-steel case, the Principle is fitted with a choice of five-link bracelet, mesh bracelet, or crocodile-textured leather strap, to complement a variety of looks.

Tags: Dial Material Oak
Description: Original aesthetics for an offbeat look. The model is available here with wood and cowhide. It is the ash tree from the vicinity of Praz-sur-Arly that is highlighted for the dial. This tree has an exceptional quality of resistance that is used in the construction of alpine chalets, the very symbol of the mountain. It is cut, sawn and worked on site. These boards, when heated, take on this beautiful caramel-brown color. For a more classic style, a mahogany Ingrassata cowhide leather strap is in harmony.

Description: The Réti Mark II is a tribute to the iconic chess game between Richard Réti and Savielly Tartakower, played in 1910. Description: RESERVOIR applies the measuring of sound to the measuring of time ! The two retrograde hands of the new RESERVOIR Sonomaster Chronograph faithfully reproduce the hands of power measurements or VU meters from analog stereo amplifiers. A tribute to the human quest for a 'pure' sound, a musical exploration full of audacity and intensity. This bi-retrograde (date and seconds at 120°) watch inaugurates the first chronograph model from RESERVOIR (central second, 30-minute counter at 12, hour counter at 6). The watch is powered by the new watchmaking caliber RSV-Bi120: a manufacture bi-retrograde ...

SPEAKE MARIN
SPEAKE MARIN Official
History
ARTICLES
Speake-Marin is an independent Swiss watch brand founded in 2002 by British watchmaker Peter Speake-Marin. The brand is renowned for its distinctive designs that blend British heritage with Swiss craftsmanship, producing luxury timepieces characterized by unique aesthetics and technical sophistication. In 2012, entrepreneur Christelle Rosnoblet acquired the company and has since served as its CEO, leading the brand towards innovative developments in haute horlogerie. Speake-Marin’s timepieces are developed and assembled in their atelier located in the Neuchâtel region of Switzerland, emphasizing their commitment to in-house movement production and meticulous craftsmanship.

Maurice Lacroix
MAURICE LACROIX Official
History
ARTICLES
Maurice Lacroix is a luxury Swiss watch brand known for its fine craftsmanship, contemporary designs, and innovative technology. The brand was established in 1975 in the Swiss town of Saignelégier, and it quickly gained a reputation for its high-quality timepieces. Today, Maurice Lacroix continues to create luxury watches that are both functional and stylish. With a focus on precision, durability, and aesthetic appeal, Maurice Lacroix is a favorite among discerning collectors and watch enthusiasts all over the world. Whether you’re looking for a classic dress watch or a sporty timepiece, Maurice Lacroix has a range of options that are sure to impress.
1975
Maurice Lacroix brand was established in the Swiss town of Saignelégier
1980
The brand focused on classic dress watches and gained a reputation for fine craftsmanship
1990
Maurice Lacroix introduced its first line of sports watches
2001
The brand launched the Pontos collection, featuring innovative technology and contemporary designs
2006
Maurice Lacroix introduced the Masterpiece collection, featuring complex, high-end movements and unique designs
2010
The brand created its first in-house movement, the ML106-7, featured in the Masterpiece Le Chronographe Squelette model
2021
The brand announced a partnership with record-breaking wingsuit pilot Jeb Corliss, creating the AIKON Venturer Limited Edition Jeb Corliss watch.

Brew Watch Co
BREW WATCH CO. Official
History
ARTICLES
The Brew watch collection is designed to celebrate and capture our enjoyable coffee experiences.
                    I've drawn inspiration from industrial espresso machines, which feature a special blend of warm
                    contrasting colors and a variety of brushing effects. This combination of colors and textures help
                    to emphasize every component that is used to create this beautiful timepiece. Similar to an espresso
                    machine, they express their own industrial nature with unique vented aesthetics seen on the sides,
                    caseback, and crown.
I was able to collectively take my passion and design process and immerse it all within the Brew collection. Ultimately, I want others to feel more connected with the daily ritual they enjoy.  It's my mission to share this story, and also to allow you to personally relate to this unique piece and remind you of the importance of time.

pages.title: Backes & Strauss
pages.content:
Backes & Strauss
 – WatchDNA
Skip to content
BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS
WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES
Item added to your cart
View cart
Check out
Continue shopping
Backes & Strauss
BACKES & STRAUSS Official
History
ARTICLES
Backes & Strauss is a venerable watch brand that represents the epitome of British luxury and craftsmanship in the world of haute horlogerie. Founded in 1789, the brand is the world’s oldest diamond company, with a rich heritage that seamlessly blends its profound expertise in diamonds with the meticulous art of watchmaking. Known for their opulent designs and unparalleled attention to detail, Backes & Strauss watches are a testament to the brand's dedication to perfection and elegance.
The brand is renowned for its masterful incorporation of exquisite diamonds into its timepieces, setting new standards in luxury watch design. Each Backes & Strauss watch is a masterpiece of precision engineering, adorned with diamonds that are carefully selected for their exceptional quality and brilliance. The brand's signature collections showcase a variety of styles, from the timeless elegance of classic designs to the innovative spirit of contemporary creations, all unified by their luxurious appeal and exceptional craftsmanship.
Backes & Strauss's commitment to excellence is evident in every aspect of their timepieces, from the intricate movements that power them to the sophisticated designs that adorn them. This dedication to quality and luxury has solidified the brand's reputation among connoisseurs and collectors alike, making Backes & Strauss synonymous with the pinnacle of diamond watchmaking.
1789
Founding of Backes & Strauss
The founding father, Georg Carl Backes was born in Steinfurth, Germany, on 12 August 1763. When he was eight years old, his family moved to Hanau, near Frankfurt. It was at Hanau, an important jewellery centre in those days, that Georg Carl first registered as a goldsmith in 1789. This marked the birth of the firm Backes & Co.
1800
London Office
By the early 1800s Georg Carl Backes was one of Hanau’s most prosperous citizens. Indeed, Backes & Co. had done so well that he sent his twenty four year-old son Johann Franz to London to open up a new office. After Georg Carl’s death in 1819, his son continued trading and manufacturing diamonds and jewellery, separating the London operation from the German one and renaming it J.F. Backes and Co.
1819
Jewellery Manufacturers
Although J.F. Backes continued to trade diamonds, the firm’s core business had, by that time, become jewellery manufacturing, for which the company had such a name that two entire pages of the 1851 Great Exhibition catalogue were devoted to its creations.
1856
Max Strauss
By now, Johann had been joined in the business by his sons, Charles and William. In 1856, the company employed an office boy by the name of Max Strauss. 15 years later, Strauss was managing J.F. Backes & Co. and in 1873, the company started trading as ‘Backes & Strauss’.
1892
Diamond and Gem Merchants
In 1892, the company decided to concentrate on diamonds. Operating as ‘Diamond and Gem merchants’, traveling was intensified from 1904 onwards; places visited included Alexandria, Vienna, Rome, Bucharest, and Paris. Our historical archives reveal the extent of the significant role Backes & Strauss provided in sourcing the finest rough diamonds, polishing and distributing them to an international clientele.
1952
Antwerp Office
In 1952, Backes & Strauss opened its office in Antwerp under the direction of Robert Lee and Gustave Ponet. Lee retired in 1986 and Vartkess Knadjian took over as the CEO. The system of diamond manufacturing and trading has continued in much the same vein as it had done during the last centuries involving a high amount of skill and perseverance. Backes & Strauss has been at the forefront of this industry. It is our history and tradition that set it apart from other diamond companies with its ability to survive, endure and innovate.
2006
Franck Muller Partnership
In the same spirit, after a management buy-out in 2003, Backes & Strauss decided to enter the luxury diamond watch market. We wanted to combine mastery in diamond-cutting with mastery in watchmaking; two crafts requiring a high amount of skill, precision and attention to detail. While searching for a partner watchmaker, we chose to work with the Franck Muller Group because of its revolutionary creative philosophy of combining tradition with modernity, an attribute Backes & Strauss shares.
2019
Family Business
Today, under the direction of Chief Executive Vartkess Knadjian, Backes & Strauss is known around the world as the ultimate diamond timepiece creator. However, this has only been the beginning; together with his son and daughter - and staying true to the company’s mission to combine tradition with modernity - Mr. Knadjian is preparing to reach new heights with Backes & Strauss.
FOUNDED
1789
HEADQUARTERS
Genthod, Switze

Partners Group
PARTNERS GROUP
History
ARTICLES
Partners Group
is a global private equity firm based in Switzerland, primarily known for managing private market investments across various sectors including private equity, real estate, infrastructure, and debt. Unlike luxury groups such as LVMH, Richemont, or the Swatch Group, Partners Group operates as an investment management firm with assets under management exceeding $100 billion. In 2022, Partners Group took over the majority of the
Breitling
shares from CVC, which had, in turn, purchased the brand in 2017. In 2023 the group acquired
Universal Geneve
, followed by
Gallet
in 2025.
Brands summary:
Founded in 1884,
Breitling
is renowned for professional-grade aviation chronographs and dive watches. Their precision instruments, like the iconic Navitimer with its distinctive slide rule bezel, blend technical functionality with bold aesthetics. In recent decades,
Breitling
has undergone a major renaissance, raising its profile by investing heavily in in-house movement production and partnering with A-list celebrities including Brad Pitt and Kelly Slater. Collectors around the world celebrated when
Breitling
announced that it was reviving
Universal Genève
, a legendary and long-dormant Swiss brand famous for its Polerouter designed by Gérald Genta and the Tri-Compax chronograph. This trajectory continued with the purchase of
Gallet
, a similarly beloved maker of classic chronographs.
FOUNDED
1996
HEADQUARTERS
Baar, Switzerland

AquaStar
History
ARTICLES
AquaStar is a Swiss luxury watchmaker specializing in retro-modern dive watches that blend historical design with contemporary engineering. Known for its heritage-inspired craftsmanship, the brand revives iconic models like the Airstar Chronograph, combining vintage aesthetics (e.g., sunburst dials, bi-directional ceramic bezels) with modern Swiss movements such as the Valjoux 7753 automatic caliber 9. AquaStar emphasizes durability and functionality, with features like 200-meter water resistance, UV-resistant materials, and collaborations with diving pioneers like Jacques Mayol, who relied on AquaStar models for record-breaking free dives 9. The brand targets diving enthusiasts, horology collectors, and professionals in maritime fields, offering timepieces that balance utility and elegance. Unique selling points include its Swiss-made precision, limited-edition reissues of mid-20th-century designs, and a commitment to preserving diving history through partnerships with explorers 9.

RSW — Rama Swiss Watch — is a Swiss watchmaker rooted in a family tradition of horology spanning more than a century. The craft was initiated by a passionate young watchmaker and later refined by his son, R. Marachly, establishing the brand in Biel/Bienne, the heart of the Swiss watch industry.
In 1983, RSW was relaunched under its own identity with a clear ambition: to create niche timepieces that stand apart through bold design and precision engineering. Now based in La Chaux-de-Fonds, the brand offers collections defined by an unwavering commitment to Swiss watchmaking excellence — from sophisticated automatic watches to sport models built for exploration.
At RSW, each timepiece is more than an instrument of measurement: it is a declaration of style, a technical work of art, and a reflection of its wearer's spirit.

Maison Boanton
History
ARTICLES
Maison Boanton is a French independent luxury watchmaker specializing in elegantly crafted mechanical wristwatches that blend timeless design heritage with contemporary refinement. The brand draws deep inspiration from classic design movements — notably the geometric precision of Art Deco and the elegant simplicity of the 1950s — to produce timepieces with balanced proportions, thoughtful details, and enduring aesthetic appeal rather than fleeting trends.
Boanton’s design philosophy centers on heritage-rooted aesthetics, artisanal craftsmanship, and technical quality. Each watch is conceived in a collaborative design studio featuring experienced designers and watchmakers, and brought to life through meticulous development, machining, finishing, and hand-assembly processes that can take up to two years per model. The brand uses quality Swiss movements — for example, La Joux-Perret automatic calibres — and durable materials such as 316L stainless steel with double-domed sapphire crystals, delivering both refined beauty and wearable performance.
Key products include the Emblème collection — a signature line with geometric cases and lacquered dials celebrating Art Deco heritage — the Héritage (a limited edition automatic with racing-inspired cues), and special pieces like the Emblème Pink Edition, created in support of the IMAGYN association for women’s cancer awareness. Maison Boanton’s target audience consists of collectors and discerning buyers who appreciate timeless design, craftsmanship, and symbolic meaning in their mechanical watches.
2024
Maison Boanton was founded by passionate watch enthusiasts and experts led by founder Hassan Sefrioui, bringing together designers with experience from Cartier and luxury watchmaking to revive heritage design in contemporary watches.

2025
Introduction of the Héritage limited edition watch — a 40 mm automatic mechanical piece powered by the La Joux-Perret G100 calibre with 68-hour power reserve, paying homage to classic automotive design and limited to 252 numbered examples.
2025
Release of the Emblème Pink Edition — a special 24-piece limited series with a pink lacquer dial in support of the IMAGYN association for breast cancer awareness, highlighting the brand’s commitment to meaningful collaborations.

2024

OISA 1937
History
ARTICLES
OISA 1937 is an Italian watch manufacture founded in Milan in 1937 by Domenico Morezzi — a master watchmaker born in 1897 who had spent 20 years at Swiss manufacturers, qualifying in Bienne, before returning to Italy to establish what would become one of the country's most significant horological enterprises. The name OISA stands for Orologeria Italiana Società Azionaria. Under Morezzi's direction, the company specialised in patented mechanical movements, eventually reaching a monthly production of 10,000 movements and a total of over 4 million watches sold.
During wartime, operations relocated temporarily outside Milan before settling permanently at Corso Como 10 in 1955. Production continued until the late 1970s, when the quartz revolution disrupted the mechanical watch market — a disruption that followed Morezzi's death in 1968 and eventually brought the original company to a close.
OISA 1937 has since been revived, with CEO Benedetto Perrotta and technical consultant Fausto Berizzi — who brings over 30 years of experience from manufactures including Frédéric Piguet, Hublot, and Vaucher — leading the project at a new facility in Albuzzano, Pavia. The revived manufacture occupies approximately 500 square metres and currently produces around 2,500 movements annually, with a long-term target of 10,000. All movements are designed in 3D CAD, machined from brass bar to tolerances in the order of microns, individually decorated, assembled, tested on a timing machine, and laser-engraved with a unique data matrix for full traceability.
"The only, true, Made in Italy" — OISA 1937 positions itself as the standard-bearer for a genuine return of in-house movement manufacturing on Italian soil.
1937
Domenico Morezzi founds OISA (Orologeria Italiana Società Azionaria) in Milan after 20 years at Swiss manufacturers, producing patented mechanical movements.
1955
OISA settles permanently at Corso Como 10 in Milan, reaching a monthly production of 10,000 movements and over 4 million total watches sold.
1968
Domenico Morezzi passes away. Production continues until the late 1970s before the quartz crisis brings operations to a close.
2020s
OISA 1937 is revived under CEO Benedetto Perrotta and technical consultant Fausto Berizzi at a new facility in Albuzzano, Pavia, producing up to 2,500 movements annually.
FOUNDED
1937

Albuzzano, Italy

Colorado Watch Company, the latest addition to the
Vortic family
represents the culmination of five years of meticulous craftsmanship and innovation.
                      We're thrilled to unveil our new line of fully modern, compact wristwatches, each powered by an American-assembled automatic movement.
                      A new brand of Vortic Watch Company, Colorado Watch Company upholds our commitment to American-made excellence. With the creation of this new brand, we asked ourselves a new question: “How much of a watch can we make in the USA while keeping the price around $1,000?” The answer is 87%. Nearly every component of these watches is American-made, especially the large metal components, like case and dial, which are machined from solid blocks of steel in our Fort Collins, Colorado manufacturing facility.
While rooted in our shared mission to create high-quality American products, this new venture allows us to reach a broader audience as well as explore new ideas we’ve had along the way. The watches are smaller and more attainable than a Vortic watch, but they’re also much more water resistant. Between our Field Watch and “The GCT” there’s something for everyone.
                    Just as with Vortic, our philosophy remains grounded in preserving and celebrating American history, one watch at a time. We're excited to invite you to explore this new chapter in American timekeeping, where precision, heritage, and innovation continue to define our legacy.

H. Moser & Cie. is a distinguished Swiss luxury watch manufacturer known for its exceptional craftsmanship and innovative designs. Founded in 1828 by Heinrich Moser in St. Petersburg, Russia, the company quickly gained recognition for producing high-quality timepieces favored by Russian aristocracy and members of the Imperial Court. In 1848, Moser returned to his hometown of Schaffhausen, Switzerland, where he established a factory that employed several hundred artisans, significantly contributing to the region’s industrialization. Today, H. Moser & Cie. operates as an independent, family-owned company, producing around 3,000 watches annually and maintaining its legacy of horological excellence.

Union Glashütte
History
ARTICLES
Union Glashütte is an affordable-luxury German watch brand rooted in the historic watchmaking town of Glashütte, Saxony. Since its founding in 1893 by Johannes Dürrstein, the brand has sought to make fine mechanical watches that bring Glashütte craftsmanship to a broader audience.
Known for blending classic, retro-modern styling with practical design, Union Glashütte combines traditionally finished mechanical movements with user-friendly aesthetics. Each timepiece is assembled, regulated and finished by hand on site in Glashütte. Though it uses Swiss-origin movements in many models, each is individually adjusted, decorated and inspected under strict German precision standards—and bears the legally protected “Made in Glashütte” designation.
1893

1997
Returned to market with Glashütte‑Original‑designed wristwatch collection and full vertical integration.
2000
Acquired by Swatch Group AG; Union placed under same group as Glashütte Original.
2008
Brand repositioned as independent with its own ateliers and dedicated mechanical collections.
2012
Opened new production facility at Frühlingsweg 5 in Glashütte.
2018
Celebrated 125‑year anniversary; special exhibition by German Watch Museum and release of limited ‘1893 Johannes Dürrstein’ edition.

MB&F
MB&F Official
Loading videos...

History
ARTICLES
MB&F is a Swiss high-end horological concept laboratory that specializes in designing and crafting small-series, avant-garde watches and mechanical art devices. Rather than following traditional watchmaking design norms, the brand’s philosophy is to fuse micro-engineering with artistic vision — producing timepieces that are more akin to three-dimensional kinetic art than conventional wristwatches.
Known for its bold, sculptural cases and technically complex movements, MB&F blends futuristic design elements (inspired by sci-fi, supercars, aviation, and architecture) with traditional Swiss watchmaking craftsmanship. Their creations appeal to collectors and connoisseurs who value originality, artistry, and mechanical innovation — essentially, watch lovers seeking wearable art rather than just time-telling instruments. MB&F’s niche lies in positioning itself not as a conventional watch brand, but as a creative “laboratory” for radical horological ideas, catering to an audience that appreciates exclusivity, engineering excellence and artistic boldness.
2005
Founding of MB&F (Maximilian Büsser & Friends) as the world’s first horological concept laboratory.
2007
Release of the first Horological Machine, HM1 — a sculptured 3-D case and engine that set the standard for future MB&F Machines.
2009
Introduction of HM3, reinforcing MB&F’s commitment to three-dimensional, unconventional watchmaking design.
2011
Launch of the Legacy Machine collection, starting with LM1 — a more classical aesthetic reinterpretation, expanding MB&F’s appeal.
2012
LM1 wins both the Public Prize and Best Men’s Watch Prize at the Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève, gaining major critical acclaim.
2015
Launch of M.A.D.Editions, MB&F’s more accessible line aiming at a wider audience while preserving creative spirit.
2015
Celebration of MB&F’s 10th anniversary; over a decade of hyper-creativity and over 11 calibres formed the base of its Horological and Legacy Machines.
2016
LM Perpetual wins the Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève Best Calendar Watch Prize — recognition for technical excellence and innovation.
2022
MB&F moves into the M.A.D.House — a century-old house transformed into a creative hub — marking a new chapter in its brand journey.
2025
20th Anniversary of MB&F, celebrating two decades since 2005 and more than 20 calibres developed over that period.
FOUNDED
2005
HEADQUARTERS
Carouge, Switzerland

Mbandf.com

Berney
History
ARTICLES
Berney
is a heritage Swiss independent watchmaker specializing in artisanal, small-series timepieces that sit at the crossroads of jewelry and horology. Founded in 1972 by watchmaker Henri Berney in L’Abbaye, Vallée de Joux, Switzerland, the brand is known for reviving and re-energizing historic watch traditions — particularly ring watches, pendant watches, and bangle watches — which blend practical timekeeping with ornamental design. Its creations are characterized by discreet elegance and meticulous Swiss precision, catering to collectors who appreciate narrative-rich objects rather than mainstream sports or luxury watches.
Berney’s design philosophy centers on craftsmanship and creative boldness that refuse to choose between mechanical tradition and decorative innovation. The maison combines old-world horological skills (like servicing vintage Valjoux chronograph movements) with modern expertise in jewelry watchmaking, producing pieces that feel both timeless and distinctive. Its creative output includes delicate ring watches with scenic motifs, refined pendants, and mechanically interesting wrist pieces, appealing to buyers who value Swiss Made quality, artisanal production, and objects with artistic resonance.
Recently, Berney has showcased its jewelry watch mastery at high profile horological events and competitions, unveiling elaborate creations such as the 2025 Panda ring watch — a fusion of Grand Feu enamel, diamonds, and quartz precision that highlights the house’s deep craft heritage and poetic design approach.
1972
Berney was founded by Henri Berney in L’Abbaye, Vallée de Joux, Switzerland, establishing an independent family watchmaking atelier focused on combining horology with jewelry objets.
2025
Berney unveiled the exceptional Panda ring watch — a masterpiece combining Grand Feu enamel, 128 diamonds, and a Swiss ETA quartz movement — illustrating its artisanal excellence and jewelry-horology fusion.
2025
The Night & Day collection was highlighted in the high horology community, featuring refined Swiss ring watches that embody poetic design and Swiss precision, crafted within the Berney family workshop.
FOUNDED
1972

Vallée de Joux, Switzerland

CITIZEN WATCH CO. LTD
CITIZEN WATCH CO. LTD
History
ARTICLES
Citizen Watch Co. Ltd.
was established by Kamekichi Yamazaki in 1918 as the Shokosha Watch Research Institute and produced its first timepiece — a pocket watch — in 1924. The name Citizen was chosen in 1930 to reflect the brand’s goal of making reliable watches at affordable prices, a legacy it continues to uphold a century later. Citizen produced Japan's first shock-resistant watch, the Parashock, in 1956, and Japan's first water-resistant watch, the Parawater, in 1959. The company’s biggest breakthrough came in 1976 with the introduction of Eco-Drive, a technology that converts both natural and artificial light into energy to power watches, eliminating the need for battery replacement. Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Citizen continued to innovate with the development of radio-controlled watches and titanium cases while refining their Eco-Drive technology. The company expanded globally through strategic acquisitions, purchasing American watch company Bulova in 2008, Swiss movement manufacturer La Joux-Perret in 2012, and Swiss watch manufacturer Frederique Constant in 2016. Today, Citizen remains one of the world's largest watch manufacturers, and is respected equally for mechanical watches like the Tsuyosa and a wide range of Eco-Drive models.
Brands summary:
Citizen
is known for its affordable, innovative timepieces along with their advances in Eco-Drive technology, Super Titanium, and satellite timekeeping. Its portfolio of brands, including
Frederique Constant
,
Bulova
,
Alpina
,
Arnold & Son
,
Campanola
and
Accutron
represents a wide variety of forms, functions, and historical legacies. At the high end is
Frederique Constant
, which was founded in 1988, and is known for offering Swiss timepieces with in-house movements — including, in recent years, a tourbillon — at relatively accessible prices.
Alpina
likewise brings Swiss-made credentials to the table with a line of professional outdoor timepieces characterized by their "4" antimagnetic, anti-shock, water-resistant, and stainless steel standards. The addition of American watchmaker
Bulova
, best known for its ties to NASA and its high-frequency movements, as well as its former sub-brand
Accutron
, which was relaunched independently in 2020, add depth and variety to the Citizen Group’s. With the acquisition of Manufacture La Joux-Perret, a respected Swiss movement-maker, Citizen Group also gained control of
Arnold & Son
, an historic English brand specializing in high-end complications.
FOUNDED
1918
HEADQUARTERS
Tokyo, Japan

Awake
History
ARTICLES
Awake Watches is a Paris-based independent watch brand (launched in 2018 by founder Lilian Thibault) that specializes in artisanal, eco-conscious timepieces. Known for blending heritage “métiers d’art” crafts with minimalist modern design, its collections (such as Son Mài lacquer-dial watches and the Summetria guilloché series) fuse vintage-inspired cases with contemporary details. Awake emphasizes sustainable materials and innovation – for example, its 2021 Mission to Earth model (made for NASA) uses a recycled-titanium case and an NFC-enabled sapphire crystal. In style, the brand can be described as a retro-modern fusion: classic watch shapes endowed with artistic, story-driven dials. This approach targets design-savvy, environmentally conscious consumers who appreciate craftsmanship and meaning in a mid-luxury watch.
Awakes’s design philosophy centers on mindfulness and meaning. Its motto “Awaken time. Awaken awareness.” reflects a mission to prompt reflection and emotional connection. The brand views watchmaking as a space where engineering meets poetry, choosing every curve and material to evoke harmony, heritage and emotion rather than just technical show. Awake’s responsible approach (built in from day one) emphasizes rarity, balance and purpose. This sincere, purpose-driven ethos resonates with a global audience of design-driven, ethically minded collectors who value artistry, storytelling and sustainability in their watches.

History
ARTICLES
Garmin Ltd. is a pioneering technology company in GPS‑based consumer electronics, specializing in navigation and wearable devices. The brand is renowned for combining practical innovation with robust design, targeting outdoor enthusiasts, athletes, pilots, mariners, and everyday users who value accuracy and durability.
Known for reliable GPS and sensor‑based products (like its iconic Forerunner running watches and Marine chartplotters), Garmin blends high‑precision engineering with user‑centered design. Its devices often feature rugged form factors, long battery life, and intuitive interfaces—all built for use in challenging environments. Garmin’s key product categories include fitness wearables, marine electronics, aviation instruments, outdoor handhelds, and automotive GPS units.
1989

History
ARTICLES
SOVRYGN is a boutique / microbrand watchmaker founded by Nigerian-Canadian designer-entrepreneur Rotimi, blending cultural storytelling with modern horological design. Their niche is in producing timepieces that go beyond mere function: they aim to imbue each watch with narrative, purpose, and aesthetic identity. Their flagship line, The Calendar, features multiple complications (day, date, month, 24-hour, power reserve) in a distinct case form that sets it apart from typical microbrand offerings. Design-wise, SOVRYGN leans toward a fusion of bold, modern geometry and layered dial architecture. They emphasize premium materials: anti-reflective sapphire crystal, exhibition case backs, BGW9 lume, and steel bracelets with micro-adjust clasps.
Their design philosophy reflects a balance of emotion and engineering — each element is intended to carry both symbolic and functional weight. Their target audience is likely watch enthusiasts who appreciate microbrands, independent storytelling, and mechanical complexity — people who don’t just see watches as tools, but as wearable expressions of identity.

Invicta Watch Group
INVICTA WATCH GROUP
History
ARTICLES
Invicta Watch Group
traces its origins to 1837, 
                                when Raphael Picard founded the company in La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland. 
                                Originally dedicated to crafting high-quality mechanical watches at accessible prices, 
                                the brand’s name—
Invicta
, meaning “invincible” in Latin—has long represented resilience and innovation in watchmaking.
After being re-established in Hollywood, Florida, in 1991 under investor
Eyal Lalo
, Invicta evolved into a global powerhouse known for its bold designs and “affordable luxury” philosophy. 
                                The company’s watches combine Swiss precision with modern flair, offering robust stainless-steel cases, 
                                flame-fusion crystals, and distinctive oversized dials.
In 2016, Invicta Watch Group expanded by acquiring the historic Swiss brand
Glycine
, 
                                reinforcing its commitment to preserving traditional horology while pursuing modern innovation. 
                                The Group also owns
TechnoMarine
and
S. Coifman
, 
                                extending its reach across luxury, sport, and lifestyle markets.
With headquarters in Florida, USA, and a continued Swiss design heritage, 
                                Invicta Watch Group today represents a fusion of craftsmanship, accessibility, 
                                and bold expression—offering over 30 collections that blend technical mastery 
                                with distinctive visual identity for collectors and enthusiasts worldwide.
FOUNDED
1837

Hollywood, Florida, USA

History
ARTICLES
Cartier is a luxury watch and jewelry brand based in France. The company was founded in 1847 by Louis-François Cartier, and it quickly became known for its high-end, elegant designs and use of precious materials. Cartier is known for its iconic Tank watch, which was first introduced in 1917, as well as its Santos watch, which was the first wristwatch designed specifically for men. The brand also offers a wide range of other styles, including dress watches, sports watches, and jewelry watches. Cartier watches are known for their precision and craftsmanship, and they are often considered status symbols. The brand has always been associated with luxury and exclusivity, it has been the choice of many kings, queens, and celebrities.

Auricoste
History
ARTICLES
Auricoste is a historic French marine and aviation watchmaker specializing in robust, precision timekeeping instruments with deep roots in military and professional use dating back to 1854. Originally focused on marine chronometers, the brand earned early prestige through rigorous standards in readability, reliability, and mechanical excellence—values that continue to define its ethos to this day. Known for iconic models like the Type 20 Flyback chronograph, Spirotechnique diver’s watch, Type 26 Aeronavale, and ScubaMaster SM300, Auricoste seamlessly blends its retro-military aesthetic with modern craftsmanship. These timepieces feature durable materials (e.g., steel 316L cases), automatic movements (ETA 2824-2, Dubois-Dépraz modules), and design elements inspired by cockpit and naval instrumentation, catering to professionals and enthusiasts who value heritage, performance, and understated style.
1854
Founded by Émile Thomas in Paris as a specialist in marine chronometers.
1900
Awarded a gold medal at the Paris Universal Exhibition for exceptional craftsmanship.
1954
Released the Type 20 flyback chronograph; over 2,000 delivered to French forces under Ministry of War specification.
1966
Collaborated with Patek Philippe to create electromechanical marine clocks and chronometers.
1980
Supplied Spirotechnique diving watches to French Navy combat swimmers and mine-clearing divers.
2001
Installed flight deck clock aboard the Charles-de-Gaulle aircraft carrier.
2010
Revisited and reissued the Type 20, creating modern reinterpretations.
2012
Re-launched the Spirotechnique diving watch in collaboration with Spirotechnique.
2019
Introduced the Type 26 aviation-inspired model.
2020
Revisited the Type 20 Flymaster model.
2021
Developed a watch for France’s demining service and created the ScubaMaster 300 (SM300).
2025
Produces around 400 watches per year; targets markets including France, South Korea, the US, and Japan.
FOUNDED
1854

History
ARTICLES
Elgé is a heritage-revival watch brand rooted in the tradition of Savoyard French watchmaking. Originally founded in Annecy in the late 1940s, the brand earned recognition for its robust—and elegantly adventurous—tool watches powered by French and Swiss mechanical movements. After ceasing operations during the quartz crisis in 1973, Elgé has made a modern comeback with renewed respect for its artisanal origins and regional craftsmanship. The brand specializes in the Chamonix, a “French-style tool watch” that melds retro design cues—like bold “Magnum” hands, generous indices, and custom serif typography—with contemporary performance features. These include a 38 mm 316L stainless-steel case, 150 m water resistance, screw-down crown and caseback, domed sapphire crystal with internal anti-reflective coating, and finishes combining brushed and polished surfaces.

History
ARTICLES
Epos is a niche Swiss mechanical watchmaker specializing in expertly crafted timepieces that blend traditional watchmaking heritage with refined, often retro‑modern styling. Known for offering “Artistry in Watchmaking”, the brand modifies and decorates Swiss base movements to deliver sophisticated complications—such as moon‑phase, big‑date, regulator, power‑reserve, and skeleton dials—at relatively accessible price points.
Their design philosophy emphasizes hand finishing, visible case‑back decoration, and in‑house modules built on vintage movements (from ETA, Sellita, Peseux, Landeron) reinterpreted into modern complications. The Artistry, Timeless, Sportive, and Pocket Watch collections showcase a fusion of technical refinement, aesthetic elegance, and mechanical authenticity.
1925
James Aubert founded James Aubert SA in the Vallée de Joux, pioneering complications like moon‑phase, power‑reserve, regulator and big‑date modules.
1983
Peter Hofer founded Montres EPOS SA (later Epos Uhren AG) in Biel/Lengnau, reviving mechanical watchmaking using vintage movements and traditional craftsmanship.
2002
Ursula Forster and Tamdi Chonge (Chonge‑Forster family) took over ownership and management, refocusing the brand on Epos‑badged mechanical collections and international expansion.
2010
Launch of the Epos 3400 model with reversed‑24‑hour second time‑zone using two independent mechanical movements.
2017
Introduction of the Epos 3435 “Verso”, the brand’s first inverted Unitas 6497 mechanical movement visible through the dial side.
2024
Debut of the stainless‑steel sports watch (Epos 3505) and its skeletonised variant (3505 SK), water‑resistant to 100 m, offering modern sporty design in the CHF ~1,600 range.
FOUNDED
1983
HEADQUARTERS
Lengnau, Switzerland

History
ARTICLES
Trauffer Watches is a Swiss‑heritage watch brand born in 2021, merging traditional Swiss folklore motifs with contemporary watch design. The company positions itself as an authentic Swiss folk‑inspired entry in watchmaking, serving a niche looking for approachable, stylish timepieces rooted in national visual identity.
The brand is built on a retro‑modern fusion aesthetic: watches showcase playful yet tasteful references to Swiss symbols—Edelweiss blossoms, cows (“Chüehli”), and Alpine charm—fixed into dials, straps, and metal embellishments.Targeted at young, culture‑rooted Swiss consumers and collectors abroad, the watches appeal to those who value folklore and heritage but also want quality, Swiss‑made craftsmanship at accessible prices. Brand ambassadors include Swiss athletes and traditional champions, reinforcing the tie to authentic Swiss identity and contemporary lifestyles.

History
ARTICLES
Shelby Watch Co. is a Canadian microbrand specializing in high-performance mechanical tool watches. Founded by Matthew McMillan, a paramedic with over 15 years of experience, the brand focuses on creating timepieces that blend rugged functionality with thoughtful design. The flagship model, the MEDIC, is a 40mm automatic watch featuring a pulsometer and asthmometer scale, catering to first responders and outdoor enthusiasts. Designed and assembled in Barrie, Ontario, Shelby Watch Co. emphasizes durability and practicality, offering watches built to withstand the rigors of daily life.
The MEDIC is crafted from 316L stainless steel, offering 150m water resistance, and is powered by the MD 7081 automatic movement with a 42-hour power reserve. Its dial features syringe hands and a bidirectional rotating bezel, all lumed with BGW9 for enhanced legibility in low-light conditions. The watch's design reflects a retro-modern fusion, combining vintage-inspired aesthetics with contemporary functionality.
2025
Shelby Watch Co. was founded by Matthew McMillan, a paramedic with over 15 years of experience, aiming to create functional and durable mechanical watches. Also the brand introduced its flagship model, the MEDIC, a 40mm automatic watch featuring a pulsometer and asthmometer scale, designed and assembled in Barrie, Ontario.

Watchcraft is a boutique art-horology brand founded and hand-operated by Eduardo Milieris in his New York City studio. Established in 1994 in Long Island City, NY, it specializes in hand-wrought, limited‑edition timepieces, bracelets, and Apple Watch bands. Each piece is sculpted from solid metals—brass, copper, sterling silver—oxidized, engraved, and then individually hand-painted, making every watch truly unique. The design philosophy centers on retro-modern fusion: embracing industrial, urban aesthetics with evolving metal patinas that age fluidly with wear. The brand melds artistic craftsmanship—welding, sculpting, painting—with functional reliability, powered by robust Citizen quartz movements.

Max Twelve
History
ARTICLES
Max Twelve is a fully independent Swiss watch brand designed and assembled in Zurich, founded by Fabian Lehner, Dipl. Ing. ETH — an engineer who set out to bring the technologies of Swiss precision engineering into watchmaking. The brand was born out of the spirit of adventure and the bravery to push beyond the status quo, operating entirely free from corporate constraints and industry convention.
Every Max Twelve watch is made to order, hand-finished, and assembled one by one in the brand's own Zurich atelier. All parts are made in Switzerland. The manufacturing process follows twelve documented steps, from a case CNC-milled from a solid block of material to titanium dials cut with precision laser technology and hand-ground before anodisation — an electrical oxidation process derived from medical industry techniques. Movement and dial mounts are milled directly into the case, eliminating conventional dial pins that could break under shock. Each case is secured with six Torx screws and sleeve nuts.
Max Twelve's design philosophy is built on engineering minimalism: "the art of designing a product as simply as possible while achieving an optimal solution with minimal components." The brand integrates technologies from medical and machine engineering, applies fair wages and responsible ESG practices, and sources its production locally in Switzerland.
"Engineering precision with bold innovation drives us to challenge the status quo and redefine the future of watchmaking."
2020
Fabian Lehner, Dipl. Ing. ETH, founds Max Twelve in Zurich with a mission to bring Swiss precision engineering technologies into independent watchmaking.
2022
Max Twelve establishes its made-to-order production model — every watch hand-finished and assembled one by one in the brand's own Zurich atelier.

History
ARTICLES
Wancher Watch is a Japanese boutique watch brand that blends traditional Japanese craftsmanship with contemporary timepiece design. Founded with a deep appreciation for time and craft, the brand focuses on creating watches that reflect Japan’s artistic heritage — especially through decorative art forms such as urushi lacquer, maki-e, raden, and other traditional techniques that appear in their high-art dial work. This unique fusion of traditional lacquer artistry with modern mechanical watchmaking gives Wancher watches both aesthetic depth and cultural significance.
The design philosophy centers on celebrating cultural identity and craftsmanship while making timepieces that are wearable and meaningful. Wancher’s collections — such as the Dream Watch Urushi Dial series — are crafted with attention to both visual artistry and performance, often featuring premium Japanese automatic movements (e.g., Miyota 9015) and 316L stainless steel cases paired with sapphire crystals. The watches aim to evoke a connection between artistic tradition and modern life, appealing to buyers who value story, culture, and handmade quality in addition to wearable watch functionality.
Wancher’s target audience includes enthusiasts of artistic and culturally inspired timepieces, those drawn to Japanese traditional art forms, and buyers seeking a standout piece that differs from typical Swiss or mainstream microbrand aesthetics. With an emphasis on handcrafted dials and artistic techniques uncommon in most watch brands, Wancher positions itself as a brand that offers unique artistic value paired with practical performance.
2005
Wancher Watch was founded by Taizo Okagaki, originally producing outdoor and performance watches for the Japanese domestic market, laying the groundwork for future artistic and culturally inspired timepieces.
2019
Wancher established its official online presence and expanded internationally, gaining global recognition beyond Japan through its website and social media platforms.
2021
The Dream Watch Collection — featuring Urushi lacquer dials crafted by master artisans — was launched via the Japanese crowdfunding platform Makuake, marking the brand’s shift toward traditional craft-centric watches.
2022
Wancher released the Dream Watch Moonphase Kaguya on Kickstarter, featuring mother-of-pearl raden and Japanese cultural themes, cementing its presence in the international watch market.
2023
The Dream Watch Urushi Dial Collection expanded with multiple traditional techniques including Hibi Nuri, Kinpaku Nuri, and Raden, in collaboration with artisans like Master Yoko Hirota.
FOUNDED
2005
HEADQUARTERS
Bungotakada, Oita Prefecture, Japan

Corum is a premium Swiss watch brand that was established in 1955 in the city of La Chaux-de-Fonds in Switzerland. The brand is well-known for the cutting-edge and forward-thinking designs it creates, as well as for its emphasis on high-quality workmanship and accurate timekeeping. The watches produced by Corum sometimes have one-of-a-kind design elements, such as limited-edition patterns, coin-shaped casings, and elaborate faces with sophisticated movements.
The firm has a rich heritage in the production of high-end timepieces and has earned a reputation as one of the most prestigious brands on the market for luxury watches. Corum provides a variety of watch designs for both men and women, such as traditional dress watches, sport watches, and designs with a more unorthodox aesthetic.

Magellan
History
ARTICLES
Magellan Watch S.A. is a Swiss watch house whose identity is rooted in a fascination with Ferdinand de Magellan — the explorer and visionary who completed the first circumnavigation of the world, forever changing our understanding of the planet. More than a timekeeper, each Magellan watch is a work of art: the brand's innovative three-dimensional concept, with curved hands following the contours of a globe, evokes exploration and the conquest of new horizons.
In 2024, Wasfi Taher, joined by his son Rami Taher, acquired Magellan Watch S.A. For this passionate horologist who has been based in Switzerland since 1990, the acquisition was more than a business decision — it was an obvious calling. A graduate in business administration from the Arab University of Beirut, Wasfi Taher quickly established strong relationships with the leading Swiss manufactures after arriving in Switzerland, becoming a respected figure in the watchmaking world.
The singular and protected aesthetic of Magellan immediately captivated Wasfi Taher, who recognised in its creations a rare emotion — combining high technical mastery with apparent simplicity. This alliance between Swiss watchmaking expertise and modern artistic expression reflects the brand's vision: to celebrate discovery and innovation while honouring tradition.
Under his leadership, Magellan Watch S.A. aspires to become a global reference, blending creative boldness with technical excellence.
1990
Wasfi Taher settles in Switzerland and builds strong relationships with the leading Swiss watch manufactures, becoming a respected figure in the watchmaking industry.
2024
Wasfi Taher and his son Rami Taher acquire Magellan Watch S.A., relaunching the Swiss watch house with its unique three-dimensional concept inspired by Ferdinand de Magellan.

COMPAGNIE FINANCIÈRE RICHEMONT SA
History
ARTICLES
Compagnie Financière Richemont SA
is one of the world's largest luxury goods conglomerates, Richemont was established in 1988 by South African businessman Johann Rupert and it now owns some of the world’s most prestigious watch and jewelry brands including Cartier, IWC Schaffhausen, and Vacheron Constantin. The company has maintained a strong focus on traditional luxury craftsmanship while adapting to modern market demands, emphasizing the distinct identity and heritage of each Maison while providing them with shared resources and expertise. Richemont is known for its long-term approach to brand management, focusing on preserving brand value and craftsmanship rather than rapid expansion. The group continues to be one of the most influential players in the global watch market.
Brands summary:
There’s a lot of depth and breadth to the brands under the Richemont umbrella, from the makers of the very first wrist-worn watch (the
Cartier
Santos) to the world’s oldest continually operating watchmaker (
Vacheron Constantin
) to historic Maisons like
Montblanc
,
Roger Dubuis
,
Piaget
,
Panerai
, and
IWC
. Then, of course, there’s Germany’s leading watchmaker
A. Lange & Söhne
, which is prized for its movements and finishing, and produces watches on par with anything coming out of Switzerland’s finest workshops. There’s also
Jaeger-LeCoultre
, often called "the watchmaker's watchmaker," which combines iconic designs like the Reverso with a spectrum of traditional watchmaking crafts including enameling and miniature painting. From the accessible luxury of
Baume et Mercier
to the intricate and whimsical creations of
Van Cleef & Arpels
, Richemont’s brands are as varied as they are historically significant. Through these diverse maisons, Richemont preserves traditional watchmaking craftsmanship while embracing innovation across different luxury price segments.

1988

Google is a premium consumer electronics brand specializing in smartwatches, particularly the Pixel Watch series. Known for seamlessly combining Google’s software intelligence with Fitbit’s health‑tracking expertise, the brand integrates circular, domed-glass designs with advanced sensors and AI‑powered functionality. Their design philosophy emphasizes elegant, minimalist aesthetics—the stainless-steel, water-resistant (5 ATM) Pixel Watch features a bold, domed display and a proprietary band‑attach mechanism inspired by camera lens mounts. This allows easy customization with a variety of woven, stretch, active, leather, and metal bands.
Key products include the first‑generation Pixel Watch (launched October 2022), Pixel Watch 2 (October 2023), and Pixel Watch 3 (September 2024), each improving health‑tracking, display area, battery life, and Wear OS integration. The audience includes tech‑savvy Android users, fitness-conscious individuals, and existing Pixel ecosystem customers seeking stylish yet powerful smartwatches with deep Google services integration.
2021
Google acquires Fitbit for USD 2.1 billion and integrates Fitbit’s health platform and team into its hardware division.
2022
Launch of Google’s first‑party Pixel Watch announced October 6 and released October 13; marks Google’s debut as a smartwatch manufacturer.
2023
Release of Pixel Watch 2 on October 12 with recycled-aluminum housing, improved sensors (heart rate, skin temperature, stress) and Wear OS 4.
2024
Pixel Watch 3 officially announced on August 13 and released Sept 10; introduced two sizes (41 mm and 45 mm), larger displays, Wear OS 5, and ‘loss of pulse’ detection.
FOUNDED
1998
HEADQUARTERS
California, United States
WEBSITE
Store.google.com/category/watches

CERTINA Official
History
ARTICLES
Certina is a Swiss watch brand that was founded in 1888. It is known for its high-quality timepieces that are designed for durability and accuracy. Certina watches feature precision movements, strong cases, and precise timing. The brand offers a range of watches for both men and women, including classic and sporty styles, as well as limited edition collections. Certina is also known for its innovative technology, such as the DS (Double Security) system, which provides extra protection for the watch’s movement. The brand is a popular choice for those who appreciate Swiss craftsmanship and precision, and it has a reputation for producing reliable and stylish timepieces.

1965
Participated in the US Navy’s Sealab II underwater project, proving the reliability of Certina DS watches under high-pressure conditions.
1971
Launched the Biostar, the world’s first watch displaying human biorhythms.
1983
Became a member of the Swatch Group, aligning with other renowned Swiss watch brands.

Anonimo is a robust, tool‑watch specialist bridging Italian elegance with Swiss precision. Born in Florence in 1997, the brand quickly became known for rugged dive watches like the Militare and Nautilo, featuring cushion‑shaped cases, signature crown placement, and high water resistance. Their aesthetic fuses retro‑modern design—think vintage cues and bronze cases—with practical functionality, targeting adventurous professionals and watch enthusiasts who value durability without sacrificing style.
With collections such as Militare, Nautilo, and the more refined Epurato, Anonimo offers timepieces powered exclusively by Swiss Made automatic Sellita movements.

TAOS
History
ARTICLES
TAOS is an independent Geneva watchmaking brand founded by Olivier Gaud in partnership with Olivier and Dominique Vaucher, whose atelier — established over 46 years ago — employs more than 40 artisans across 15 different trades and sits at the centre of Geneva's world of exceptional horological creation. The brand was born from a shared passion to promote and transcend the Métiers d'Arts, and its name embodies three pillars: excellence, humility, and creativity.
Olivier Gaud's path to founding TAOS is one of rare immersion. Born in Switzerland in 1985, he developed his passion for watches at 20, studied horology under Philippe Cantin, and spent seven years at Christie's Geneva examining and cataloguing thousands of timepieces. His career also included positions at Cartier in London and Geneva. Before TAOS, he founded the OLIGO brand in 2021.
Every TAOS watch is a unique piece — "each dial is a work of art." The movements are exclusively produced by Télôs Watch, which developed a bespoke caliber (VOP318) for TAOS: an automatic movement with approximately 72 hours of power reserve, with bridges designed specifically to accommodate elaborate engraving work. All technical construction and casing is handled by Geesco, a Geneva atelier responsible for machining, stone-setting, and finishing from design plans through to final assembly.
TAOS watches are produced in micro-quantities in Geneva by the finest Swiss artisans. Each creation is described as essentially artistic — free from conformist constraints, born from the complete mastery of the workshop and the creative freedom that mastery enables.
2021
Olivier Gaud founds the OLIGO brand, preceding TAOS and deepening his creative and commercial experience in independent watchmaking.
2022
TAOS is created through the partnership of Olivier Gaud with Atelier Olivier Vaucher, bringing together Gaud's vision and Vaucher's 46-year legacy of Geneva artisanal watchmaking.
2023
Télôs Watch delivers the exclusive VOP318 caliber — an automatic movement with ~72h power reserve, with bridges designed for elaborate engraving — produced solely for TAOS.
HEADQUARTERS
Geneva, Switzerland

LaCité
History
ARTICLES
La Cité Watches is a Canadian watch brand founded in Montréal by Olivier and Frédéric — two global citizens united by a shared passion for travel, design, and the art of measuring time. Olivier, with a background in advertising and creative direction, and Frédéric, a serial web entrepreneur with over 25 years of experience and a lifelong love of exploration, set out to create timepieces that speak the language of the modern nomad.
The brand's philosophy is rooted in movement: "We don't wear time. We move through it." Every watch is conceived for those who live at the intersection of cities and time zones — the professionals, explorers, and adventurers who call the world their home. La Cité calls this universe the "Global Playground," and every detail of their watches is designed to serve it.
La Cité's flagship collection, the Urbanite GMT, is a dual-timezone automatic timepiece hand-assembled and tested in their Montréal facility. Built for durability and refined aesthetics, it draws inspiration from the cities that define modern life — their architecture, their rhythms, and their people. Limited edition variants like the Montréal Edition celebrate the brand's hometown roots while honoring a global outlook.
With worldwide shipping, duties included, and a 24-month warranty on every timepiece, La Cité Watches backs its craftsmanship with a commitment as solid as the watches themselves. For those who are always between departures and arrivals, La Cité is the watch that keeps up.
2020
La Cité Watches is founded in Montréal by Olivier and Frédéric, with a mission to create watches for global citizens and urban explorers.
2021
Launch of the Origin GMT, the brand debut timepiece — a dual-timezone automatic watch dedicated to world travelers.
2022
Introduction of the Urbanite GMT collection, expanding the lineup with new dial designs inspired by cities around the world.
2022
La Cité establishes its Montréal assembly and testing facility, ensuring every watch is hand-finished and quality-checked in Canada.
2023
Release of the Montréal Limited Edition — a tribute to the brand home city, combining local pride with global design sensibility.
2023
La Cité launches worldwide shipping with duties and import fees included, making their watches accessible to collectors on every continent.
2024
The brand grows its international community of Global Playground enthusiasts, cementing its identity as the watch of the modern urban nomad.
FOUNDED
2020

Montréal, Canada

Atelier Nossedh
History
ARTICLES
Atelier Nossedh is a Scandinavian-influenced independent watch microbrand specializing in vintage-inspired mechanical timepieces with contemporary execution. The brand was founded by Alexander Gimell, a Swedish designer driven by a passion for classic chronograph design and a desire to create a watch that felt just right — balanced, elegantly proportioned, and mechanically honest. The name Nossedh means “passing time” in the Sámi language, reflecting the brand’s philosophical connection to the art and experience of timekeeping.
Atelier Nossedh’s design philosophy prioritizes proportion, comfort, and purposeful details. Its debut model — the AN.01 Chronograph — is Swiss-made and inspired by mid-20th-century chronographs but avoids imitation. It features a compact 37 mm case, a bi-compax dial layout with a tachymeter scale, a manual-wind Sellita SW510 movement with ~63 hours of power reserve, and double-domed sapphire crystal. This blend of classic watchmaking cues and ergonomic refinement creates a watch that balances everyday wearability with vintage charm.
2025
Atelier Nossedh was founded by Swedish designer Alexander Gimell as an independent microbrand focused on classic chronograph design and refined watch proportions, drawing from Scandinavian design influences.
2025
The brand announced its debut model, the AN.01 vintage-inspired chronograph — a Swiss-made mechanical watch powered by the Sellita SW510 manual-wind calibre, available in multiple dial colours.
2025
Pre-orders for the AN.01 opened in late 2025 via the brand’s website, marking Atelier Nossedh’s first limited-batch production and direct-to-consumer launch period.
FOUNDED
2025

History
ARTICLES
Wenger is a heritage Swiss brand in watches and travel/business gear, with roots stretching back to 1893 in the Jura region of Switzerland. Originally famed as one of two official makers of the Swiss Army Knife, Wenger has since refocused its identity post‑2005 as a specialist in precision watches and intelligently designed luggage and backpacks—offering Swiss craftsmanship at accessible price points.
Known for its reliable, functional and stylish design language, Wenger marries a retro‑modern aesthetic with practical features—for instance, ergonomic compartments, durable materials, and clean Swiss design influences. Its products include collections like City Classic, Vintage Sport, Seaforce in watches, and well‑engineered business/backpack gear like the Pegasus, Synergy and City Traveler series. The brand targets active urban professionals and travelers who value everyday functionality combined with polished, minimalist styling—a demographic that appreciates Swiss reliability without a luxury‑price.
1893
Founded as Paul Boéchat & Cie knife factory in Courtételle near Delémont in the Swiss Jura region.
1897
Theo Wenger joins as leader and renames the firm Wenger & Cie, later Wenger S.A.
1908
Awarded half of the official Swiss Army knife contract alongside Victorinox.
1997
Wenger begins producing Swiss‑made watches, expanding beyond cutlery.
2005
Acquired by Victorinox after financial difficulties, but retained distinct identity for watches and travel gear.
2013
Victorinox integrates Wenger knife lines into its own Victorinox collection; Wenger focuses solely on watches and travel/business gear.
2016
Brand relaunched at Baselworld, adopting slogan 'A Swiss Company Since 1893' and emphasising value‑driven Swiss design.
2017
Introduction of the Wenger‑branded 'Wenger Red' Swiss Army Knife, produced in Delémont under Victorinox.
FOUNDED
1893

Canton of Jura, Switzerland

S. Coifman
History
ARTICLES
S. Coifman is a heritage-luxury niche watch brand specializing in Swiss-crafted timepieces with a strong emphasis on artisanal workmanship and classical design. Known for its origin in a small music-box workshop in Chiasso, Switzerland, where founder Simon Coifman perfected his first 17-jewel gold-plated watch movement in 1906. The brand combines rich historical roots and fine mechanical tradition with accessible luxury pricing (after its modern revival) and hand-assembly of its watches.
Its design philosophy centres on “craftsman watch” ideals — refined, detailed finishings, complications for the enthusiast (e.g., tourbillons and chronographs in its early years) and classic styling for discerning wearers. The key product lines include dress and sport-elegant models that appeal to both collectors looking for Swiss heritage and buyers seeking handcrafted quality at a more attainable price. The target audience is the watch lover or collector who values Swiss horological history and craftsmanship, but may not be willing to pay the very high premiums of ultra-luxury brands. S. Coifman’s unique selling points are its genuine Swiss origins (Chiasso workshop), its revival under modern management (acquired by the Invicta Watch Group in 2004) and its positioning as a “hand-built” timepiece made for the connoisseur.
1906
Founder Simon Coifman crafted his first watch movement in a Chiasso, Switzerland workshop — 17-jewel, gold-plated, marking the origin of the brand.
1907
S. Coifman began crafting timepieces for local artisans (metal-workers, stonemasons, copper-smiths) in Chiasso, earning reputation as the “craftsman watch”.
1953
S. Coifman was commissioned to create an 18 k gold mechanical chronograph for select collectors — numbered pieces with hand-engraved components.
2004
The brand was acquired by the Invicta Watch Group and its heritage revived — operations still reference the Chiasso roots while modernising the brand.
FOUNDED
1906

Florida, USA

History
ARTICLES
Alexander Shorokhoff is a German luxury watch manufacturer specializing in artistic mechanical timepieces
. Founded by Alexander Shorokhov in 2003 in Alzenau, Bavaria
, the brand creates limited-edition watches that blend elaborate, hand-finished design with modern functionality. Each timepiece is crafted in Germany from high-quality materials and features unique dial art or engravings inspired by famous artworks and historical motifs
. These “art on the wrist” watches combine avant-garde, often retro-inspired aesthetics with precise Swiss/German movements. Produced in small series (often 30–100 pieces), they carry a special exclusivity that makes them coveted by collectors and connoisseurs worldwide.
The brand’s design philosophy draws on founder Alexander Shorokhov’s Russian artistic heritage and training in architecture, merging creative flair with German precision.
1992
Alexander Shorokhov founded a Poljot watch distribution company in Germany.
1994
He established the Poljot-International watch brand.
2003
Alexander Shorokhoff Uhrenmanufaktur was founded by Alexander Shorokhov in Alzenau, Germany.
2012
The company began participating in international watch competitions and has received design nominations and awards every year since then.

Dior
CHRISTIAN DIOR Official
History
ARTICLES
Dior is a world-renowned watch brand that has been synonymous with luxury and elegance since its establishment in 1947. Founded by Christian Dior, a pioneer of haute couture, the brand quickly became known for its exquisite designs and impeccable craftsmanship, setting a new standard in the fashion industry.
With its extensive range of watches, Dior offers a wide variety of styles and designs that cater to the tastes and preferences of its discerning clientele. Whether you’re looking for a classic, timeless piece or a modern, avant-garde style, Dior has something to suit every occasion and personality.
What sets Dior watches apart is their unwavering commitment to quality and attention to detail. Each timepiece is crafted to perfection by master watchmakers using the finest materials, and features intricate movements and stunning dial and case designs that showcase the brand’s creativity and innovation.
Dior watches are not just fashion accessories, but true works of art that embody the brand’s values of excellence, sophistication, and timelessness. With a Dior watch on your wrist, you are not just keeping time, but making a statement about your style and taste. A symbol of luxury and elegance, a Dior watch is the perfect accessory for those who appreciate the finer things in life.
1947
Christian Dior launches his eponymous fashion house in Paris, France.
1967
The first Dior watches are produced by the company's licensing partner, Benedom.

Dior establishes its own watch division, and the first collection of Dior watches is introduced.
2005
Dior introduces the "Christal" watch, which features a sapphire crystal bracelet and a unidirectional rotating bezel.
2011

LÖBNER
History
ARTICLES
LÖBNER is a German watch manufacturer with a history that begins in Prussia in 1862, when founder Franz Ludwig Löbner established the company under a principle that would define it for generations: "Vom Guten das Beste" — The Best of the Good. From its earliest years, LÖBNER was a pioneer in precision timekeeping technology, producing high-precision stopwatches capable of measuring time intervals to one-hundredth of a second as early as 1881, and to one-thousandth of a second by 1892.
The brand's contributions to timekeeping history are extraordinary. In 1894, LÖBNER supplied a clock system with a perpetual calendar for the German Reichstag. From the mid-1920s onward, LÖBNER became a pioneer of modern sports timekeeping — developing the world's first timekeeping system with a time printer, and deploying its electromechanical systems at the Olympic Games in Amsterdam, Lake Placid, and Berlin 1936. In the 1930s, LÖBNER documented the legendary world record of Rudolf Caracciola, who set the longest course world record in history at 432.7 km/h.
After decades of historically significant timings, the brand disappeared. Seventy-nine years later, in 2023, LÖBNER was revived — with two collections: the LÖBNER Sledge three-hand watch and the LÖBNER Steelracer chronograph. The revival is accompanied by "Vom Guten das Beste," a historically precise book on the company's history from 1862 to 1944, published in a limited edition of 200 copies in German and 200 in English.
As a 1912 commemorative publication noted: "LÖBNER created clocks that scientifically did what was expected of them, namely exactly determine the fifths, tenths, hundredths and thousandths of a second."
1862
LÖBNER is founded in Prussia by Franz Ludwig Löbner under the motto "Vom Guten das Beste" — The Best of the Good.
1881
LÖBNER produces high-precision stopwatches capable of measuring time intervals to one-hundredth of a second.
1892
LÖBNER develops a three-metre-high military clock capable of measuring to one-thousandth of a second.
1894
LÖBNER supplies a clock system with a perpetual calendar for the German Reichstag.
1928
LÖBNER develops the world's first timekeeping system with a time printer, deployed at the Olympic Games in Amsterdam.
1932
LÖBNER's electromechanical timekeeping system is used at the Winter Olympic Games in Lake Placid.
1930s
LÖBNER documents Rudolf Caracciola's legendary world record of 432.7 km/h — the longest course world record in history.
1936
LÖBNER serves as official timekeeper at the Olympic Games in Berlin.
2023
LÖBNER is revived, launching two collections: the Sledge three-hand watch and the Steelracer chronograph. The brand history book "Vom Guten das Beste" is published in a limited edition of 400 copies.
FOUNDED
1862

Germany

L. Leroy
History
ARTICLES
L. Leroy is a French haute horlogerie Maison with over 240 years of uninterrupted history. Founded in Paris in 1785 by Charles Leroy — the son of a watchmaker — the Maison was officially appointed watchmaker to the French royal family from its very inception, and later became a supplier to the imperial family. In 1835, L. Leroy was appointed watchmaker to the French Ministry of the Navy, subsequently supplying the most prestigious European naval fleets for nearly a century and a half. The Maison's crowning achievement came in 1900 when Louis Leroy presented the "Leroy 01" at the Paris Universal Exhibition, where it was awarded the Grand Prix — a piece so technically sophisticated it remained the absolute benchmark for highly complicated watchmaking until 1989. Today, L. Leroy timepieces are entirely manufactured in Geneva, embodying a philosophy devoted to the art of high complications, métiers d'art, and gem-setting. The Maison also provides a full restoration service for vintage and antique watches, dedicated to private collectors and museums.
1785
Charles Leroy founds the Maison in Paris. From its inception, L. Leroy is officially appointed watchmaker to the King of France.
1835
L. Leroy is appointed watchmaker to the French Ministry of the Navy, beginning nearly 150 years of supply to the world's most prestigious naval fleets.
1900
Louis Leroy presents the "Leroy 01" at the Paris Universal Exhibition. The timepiece wins the Grand Prix and becomes the global standard for highly complicated watchmaking.
1989
After nine decades, the "Leroy 01" finally cedes its status as the absolute benchmark for high complications — a record unmatched in watchmaking history.
2025
Celebrating 240 years of craftsmanship, L. Leroy launches the Osmior BAL DU TEMPS at Geneva Watch Days — a flying tourbillon and minute repeater embodying the pinnacle of contemporary haute horlogerie.
FOUNDED
1785
