Haute-Rive
History
ARTICLES
Haute-Rive is an independent Swiss watchmaking brand founded by Stéphane von Gunten, a watchmaker from La Chaux-de-Fonds who carries more than three generations of watchmaking in his family. The brand takes its name from the ancestral atelier of Irénée Aubry — von Gunten's great-great-grandfather — who operated his workshop at Haute-Rive in Chez-le-Bart, and whose inventions became some of the most celebrated in 19th-century horology.
Irénée Aubry was known to his neighbours simply as "the inventor." In 1887, he created an exceptional pocket watch for the Jura canton's gift to Pope Leo XIII — a timepiece capable of running 40 days without winding, unprecedented at the time. In 1888 he received the Cross for the Church and papal recognition, followed by success at the 1889 Paris Exposition Universelle. His most enduring invention came with patent no. 88, filed January 10, 1889: an eight-day pocket watch that became one of watchmaking's major commercial successes between 1890 and 1930 under the name Hebdomas, winning prizes at multiple World Expositions.
Stéphane von Gunten spent nearly twenty years working for prestigious watchmaking houses before establishing Haute-Rive as an independent brand — a decision he describes as extracting himself from the time constraints of large groups to find complete freedom of expression. He crafts each watch individually, in a traditional manner, as both engineer and visionary: a self-described humble innovator, conscious of the limits of his art and working to push them back.
The brand's philosophy — haute horlogerie, tradition et innovation — is embodied in its flagship creation, the Honoris Prima: a wristwatch that holds a world record for power reserve, achieving 1,000 hours of autonomy with a single mainspring barrel and tourbillon. It is an hommage aux valeurs familiales, à l'humain, au partage — honouring family values, humanity, and the transmission of craft across generations.
1887
Irénée Aubry, ancestor of Stéphane von Gunten, creates a pocket watch running 40 days without winding as a gift from the Jura canton to Pope Leo XIII.
1888
Aubry receives the Cross for the Church and papal recognition, then achieves international acclaim at the 1889 Paris Exposition Universelle.
1889
Aubry files patent no. 88 for an eight-day pocket watch — the Hebdomas — which becomes one of the major watchmaking successes between 1890 and 1930, winning prizes at multiple World Expositions.
2020s
Stéphane von Gunten revives the Haute-Rive name after nearly twenty years in prestigious watchmaking houses, establishing an independent brand in La Chaux-de-Fonds.
2024
The Honoris Prima sets a world record for wristwatch power reserve — 1,000 hours with a single mainspring barrel and tourbillon.
HEADQUARTERS
La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland

Etien
History
ARTICLES
Etien was founded by Tien, inspired by a transformative experience at the Fabergé Egg Exhibition in China in 2016. Immersed in the intricate details and timeless allure of Fabergé's masterpieces, Tien found the inspiration to create something equally enchanting. The exhibition, showcasing exquisite craftsmanship and opulent design, left a lasting impression on Tien. Each piece's meticulous artistry and historical significance stirred a deep appreciation for traditional craftsmanship and artistry.
This pivotal moment ignited a creative spark within Tien, driving an ambition to create something that could similarly captivate and endure through time. Tien embarked on a journey to marry the best of traditional craftsmanship with cutting-edge technology, aiming to forge a unique path in the world of horology. The vision was clear: to design and produce watches that are not just functional timepieces but true embodiments of refined taste and enduring beauty.
Each ETIEN watch is crafted with meticulous attention to detail, ensuring that every piece reflects the blend of classic elegance and modern innovation. Through this harmonious fusion, Tien aspires to bring forth creations that honor traditional craftsmanship while embracing the advancements of contemporary technology. The goal is to offer timepieces that stand as symbols of sophistication, appealing to those who appreciate the finer things in life.

Mathey Tissot
History
ARTICLES
Mathey Tissot is an independent Swiss watchmaker specializing in affordable luxury timepieces. Established in 1886 by Edmond Mathey-Tissot in Les Ponts-de-Martel, Switzerland, the brand initially gained recognition for its precision in crafting repeater pocket watches and chronographs. Over the years, Mathey Tissot has built a reputation for producing Swiss-made watches that combine classic design elements with modern functionality, offering enthusiasts a blend of heritage and contemporary appeal.
The brand's design philosophy emphasizes timeless aesthetics, often drawing inspiration from vintage styles while incorporating modern materials and movements. Key products include chronographs, moonphase models, and military-inspired designs, catering to both men and women. Mathey Tissot's target audience comprises watch enthusiasts and collectors seeking quality Swiss craftsmanship at accessible price points, distinguishing itself from more mainstream luxury brands.
1886

1969-1970
Elvis Presley purchased several dozen customized Mathey-Tissot automatic watches, gifting them to family, friends, and staff.
2014
Alberto Frigerio acquired Mathey-Tissot, bringing renewed focus on quality and personalized service.
2017

Ateliers deMonaco is a Swiss high‑end watch manufacture founded in 2008, blending Monaco’s regal heritage with traditional Swiss craftsmanship. Its credo, “Noblesse Oblige”, underlines a commitment to timeless excellence and independence. The brand designs haute‑horlogerie timepieces that marry Monaco-inspired elegance and Swiss “Geneva Seal” quality. Each watch reflects Monaco’s contemporary beauty and the strict “Swiss Made” tradition, executed with fine workmanship, emotion and rarity.
Ateliers deMonaco is known for its in‑house mechanical movements (including proprietary tourbillons and a flyback chronograph) and lavish hand‑finishing. Its collections span six distinct series, for example, complicated tourbillon regulators, an Admiral Flyback chronograph, and Geneva Seal‑certified models. Many models carry the prestigious Poinçon de Genève, reflecting an exceptional standard of finishing.

                          The collection pays homage to the determination and agility of the Tiger, the majestic emblem that symbolizes the PLEIN SPORT universe. It features a range of bold, iconic watches designed to defy conventional norms with their fearless aesthetics.

AL-Time watches

History
ARTICLES
AL-Time is an independent watch brand built around a single, quietly radical idea: that the most valuable thing a watch can tell you is not the hour, but what to do with it. The name is rooted in chemistry — AL-13, the periodic table designation for aluminum, the ultra-lightweight metal at the heart of every AL-Time case. Lightweight by design, contemporary in spirit, and affordable by intention, AL-Time positions itself as a status symbol of a new kind — one that signals not wealth, but awareness.
Every AL-Time dial carries a signature detail that sets it apart from anything else in the market: the number 13. Placed as an extra hour marker, it serves as a daily reminder to claim an additional hour for the things that matter most — the pursuits, passions, and people that make time worth measuring. It is a design choice that transforms the watch from an instrument of obligation into one of permission.
The brand's collections — Leger, Courage, and Rebel — each express a different facet of this philosophy, from the effortlessly light to the boldly intentional. The Le Coup Automatique adds a mechanical heartbeat to the lineup, bringing the brand's values to life through the movement itself. The tagline says it plainly: "Time is luxury — Use it mindfully."
For a generation redefining what it means to live well, AL-Time offers a watch that fits the wrist with zero weight and fits the life with zero compromise.
2019
AL-Time is founded by Alex Wiederin and Tino Valentinitsch, launching with the Leger, Courage, and Rebel collections — all built around A6063 Grade Aluminium cases.
2020
Le Coup Automatique is introduced in December 2020 — AL-Time first mechanical automatic movement offering, with hacking seconds feature.
FOUNDED
2019

AL-Time.com

History
ARTICLES
Tiffany & Co. is a luxury jewelry and watch brand based in the United States. The company was founded in 1837 by Charles Lewis Tiffany and John B. Young. The brand is known for its high-end jewelry and watches, as well as for its timeless, classic designs. Tiffany & Co. watches are known for their elegant and refined style, often featuring precious materials such as gold and diamonds. The brand is particularly well-known for its Atlas collection, which features watches with a large Roman numeral dial, and the Tiffany East West collection, which is inspired by the Art Deco era. The brand is also known for its commitment to sustainability and ethical practices. Tiffany & Co. watches are highly sought-after by collectors and enthusiasts for their craftsmanship, style and exclusivity.

Gérald Genta
History
ARTICLES
Gérald Genta is a prestigious Swiss haute horlogerie brand rooted in the visionary work of legendary watch designer Gérald Charles Genta. Known historically as one of the most influential figures in modern watchmaking, Genta designed some of the industry’s most iconic watches — including the Audemars Piguet Royal Oak and Patek Philippe Nautilus — before establishing his eponymous brand in 1969 to bring his own creative visions to life.
The brand combines bold aesthetic innovation with high watchmaking craftsmanship, emphasizing avant-garde design, complicated mechanical movements, and artistic expression. Early Gérald Genta creations included groundbreaking designs and horological innovations such as the ultra-thin automatic perpetual calendar and Grande Sonnerie — a world-class chiming wristwatch developed over several years.
1969
Gérald Genta founded his eponymous brand with a mission to create extraordinary watches that reflected his personal artistic vision, after years of designing legendary models for other major manufacturers.
2000
The original Gérald Genta company was sold (and ultimately integrated into Bulgari), marking the end of its initial independent era.
2011
Gérald Genta, the founder and visionary behind the brand and countless horological icons, passed away, leaving behind thousands of designs and an enduring legacy.
2023
Gérald Genta was relaunched under **LVMH’s La Fabrique du Temps Louis Vuitton** atelier with access to Genta’s archives and guidance from master watchmakers, focusing on high watchmaking and limited productions.
FOUNDED
1969

History
ARTICLES
Jowissa is a Swiss watch brand specializing in elegant, Swiss-made timepieces that blend traditional craftsmanship with contemporary design. Founded in 1951 by Josef Wyss in Bettlach, Switzerland, Jowissa has evolved into a third-generation family-run business. The brand is renowned for its signature faceted crystal designs, which add a touch of brilliance to each watch, and its commitment to offering high-quality Swiss watches at accessible prices. Jowissa's collections cater to both men and women, featuring a range of styles from classic to modern, ensuring there's a timepiece for every taste.
The design philosophy of Jowissa emphasizes bold aesthetics and vibrant colors, aiming to make a statement on the wrist. Each watch is assembled by hand in Switzerland, ensuring meticulous attention to detail and adherence to Swiss quality standards. The brand's dedication to innovation is evident in its use of unique materials and design techniques, such as the incorporation of cut glass reminiscent of gemstones, which has been a hallmark since the 1960s. Jowissa's watches are not just timekeepers but also fashion accessories that reflect personal style and sophistication.

History
ARTICLES
GEOVANI is a Swiss luxury watch brand created in 2003 as the high-end line of the Riba Watch Group. Based in La Chaux-de-Fonds — in the canton of Neuchâtel, at the heart of the Swiss Jura watchmaking valley — the brand was born from the growing success and demand for RIBAWATCH models, positioning itself as the group's premium offering with a focus on luxury, exclusivity, and timeless design.
The GEOVANI collection emphasizes refined finishes, high-quality jewels set inside and outside the dial, and Swiss-made craftsmanship with water resistance up to 5 ATM. Initially presented across several diamond-set ranges primarily for women, the brand was built with the ambition of earning recognition among established luxury watch manufacturers.
At its core, GEOVANI is driven by three values: trust in the expertise of its atelier professionals, diversity through varied annual collections of quality timepieces, and the passion of a team deeply committed to their craft — a passion they aim to share through every creation.

History
ARTICLES
Bouveret France is a distinguished luxury artisanal atelier specializing in watch straps and leather goods. Founded by Gérard Bouveret in 1987 following over a decade of apprenticeship within the Rochet Group, the company reflects a deep heritage rooted in the Hauts Doubs—a historic cradle of French watchmaking. Renowned for its master leather craftsmanship, Bouveret combines timeless artisanal techniques with exceptional materials. Its workshops—now part of Rochet Group’s luxury Made in France production—execute around 60 distinct manual operations per piece, transforming noble skins (such as alligator, ostrich, lizard, python, galuchat, and calf) into elegant, durable, and soulful creations. Bouveret's offerings cater both to high-end watchmakers and discerning jewelers, targeting clientele who value craftsmanship, customization, and French-made quality.
1987
Gérard Bouveret, after over a decade apprenticing at Rochet Group, founded Gerard Bouveret Creation in Hauts Doubs, the historic French watchmaking region.
2017
Approximately 30 years later, Bouveret reintegrated into Rochet Group, becoming its luxury Made in France production unit.
2021
Rochet Group acquired Bouveret, integrating its high-end leather craftsmanship into the group's offerings.
2024
Bouveret France merged with Sibra under Rochet Group to form Sibra Bouveret Manufacture, consolidating luxury bespoke and first-equipment leather strap production in Besançon.

ALTO
ALTO WATCHES

History
ARTICLES
ALTO — an acronym for Arts Limited Time Objects — is an independent watch brand built on a premise as rare as it is compelling: that art and time are inseparable, and that a timepiece should be the ultimate expression of that relationship. Where most watchmakers celebrate mechanical heritage or technical innovation, ALTO invites its wearers into a deeper conversation — one where the arts, in all their forms, shape our perception of time itself. Every ALTO watch is conceived not merely as an instrument of measurement, but as a wearable artistic object that carries meaning beyond its function.
The brand's collections reflect this philosophy with clarity and intentionality. From the architectural restraint of the Monochrome Black and Monochrome Grey editions to the evocative Falcon Eye — each model represents a distinct artistic vision translated into the language of horology. Limited in production by design, ALTO's pieces are created for collectors who understand that true luxury lies in the singularity of an object and the story it embodies. The name itself — Arts Limited Time Objects — encodes this ethos: these are not mass-produced timepieces, but curated statements.
With a growing presence on the international independent watch scene, ALTO continues to push the boundaries of what a contemporary watch brand can represent. By fusing artistic ambition with watchmaking craft, the brand occupies a unique position in the landscape of modern horology — one where the question is never simply "what time is it?" but always "what does time mean to you?"
HEADQUARTERS
France

Altowatches.com

Samsung is a global electronics leader specializing in wearable devices—particularly the Samsung Galaxy Watch series, sold in Canada through its official channel. The brand emphasizes a sleek fusion of modern technology and classic watch aesthetics, combining Samsung’s hallmark circular bezel designs with cutting‑edge smart features like AI‑powered wellness tracking and smart home integration.
As a pioneer in health‑focused wearables, Samsung offers devices that blend performance with style. Its latest models—such as the Galaxy Watch8 Classic and Ultra—feature high‑performance 3 nm processors, rotating bezels, and seamless integration with Samsung Health and SmartThings for fitness, sleep coaching, and smart‑device control. Samsung targets tech‑savvy consumers who want the functionality of a smartwatch with the look and feel of a timeless timepiece.

History
ARTICLES
Jaipur Watch Company is an Indian artisanal watchmaker specializing in handcrafted luxury timepieces that transform historical artifacts, antique coins, and cultural heritage into functional art. Known for its "storytelling through watches" philosophy, the brand combines reclaimed materials (pre-independence coins, vintage typewriter keys, heritage wood) with Swiss/Japanese movements, creating historically significant wearable pieces. Key collections include Coin Watches (British Raj-era coins), Typewriter Key Watches (repurposed keys), and Art Deco-inspired designs, targeting history enthusiasts, collectors, and conscious luxury consumers who value cultural preservation and uniqueness. The brand’s design ethos centers on reviving forgotten narratives – each piece documents India’s heritage through materials like wood from 200-year-old havelis (mansions) or Partition-era artifacts. Unique selling points include limited-edition craftsmanship (each watch numbered), sustainable luxury (upcycled materials), and personalized provenance tracking.
2013
Founded by Gaurav Mehta in Jaipur, launching India's first micro-luxury watch brand with a focus on transforming antique coins into wearable history.
2015
Introduced the signature 'Coin Watch' collection using authentic British India-era silver coins, gaining recognition for cultural preservation.
2017
Launched the 'Typewriter Series', repurposing vintage typewriter keys into watch dials. Featured at India Design ID, establishing art-meets-horology niche.
2018

Received international acclaim when Prime Minister Narendra Modi gifted a custom 'Pride of India' coin watch to Denmark's PM.
2021
Launched 'Partition Series' using artifacts from 1947 India-Pakistan Partition, documenting refugee heritage through horology.
2023
Debuted 'Astrolabe Collection' featuring astronomical instrument designs and collaborations with Indian astronomers.
2024
Opened experiential atelier in Jaipur, offering watchmaking workshops and material provenance tours.

Milus
History
ARTICLES
Milus is a Swiss watchmaking house founded in 1919 by Paul William Junod in Biel/Bienne, Switzerland — a brand whose history spans more than a century of design innovation, cultural recognition, and quiet independence. Refined and minimalistic in harmony with its legacy since 1919, Milus aims to enhance its close affinity with today's modern individual.
Under Paul Herbert Junod, who took over in 1951, the company grew rapidly — production increased by 200% and the workforce expanded from 35 to 50 employees. In 1962, a modernist headquarters designed by architect Edouard Lanz was completed in Bienne. Throughout the 1970s, Milus won the prestigious Rose d'Or de Baden-Baden three consecutive times (1970, 1971, 1973) for models including La Mer, Pocket Watch, and Avant-garde. In 1972, the brand co-founded Ditronic SA with four other watchmakers to produce the first electronic LCD watches in Switzerland.
By the 1980s and 1990s, Milus watches were displayed in world museums including MoMA in New York. In 2000 the brand was nominated among the "50 brands of Swiss watchmaking in the 20th century." A notable chapter in the brand's heritage: the Snow Star watch was issued to US Navy pilots as part of survival kits.
In 2019, on its centenary, Milus relaunched under the leadership of Luc Tissot with the LAB and ICONIC collections. In 2024, the brand received the Switzerland Prestige Award for Luxury Watch Brand of the Year.
1919
Milus is founded by Paul William Junod in Biel/Bienne, Switzerland.
1951
Paul Herbert Junod takes over; production increases 200%, workforce grows from 35 to 50 employees.
1962
Modernist headquarters designed by architect Edouard Lanz completed in Bienne.
1970
Milus wins the Rose d'Or de Baden-Baden — a recognition it receives three times between 1970 and 1973 for models including La Mer, Pocket Watch, and Avant-garde.
1972
Milus co-founds Ditronic SA with four other watchmakers to produce the first electronic LCD watches in Switzerland.
1986
Milus watches begin appearing in world museums, including MoMA in New York — a distinction maintained through 1999.
2000
Milus is nominated among the "50 brands of Swiss watchmaking in the 20th century."
2019
Centenary relaunch under Luc Tissot with the LAB and ICONIC collections.
2024
Milus receives the Switzerland Prestige Award for Luxury Watch Brand of the Year.
FOUNDED
1919

History
ARTICLES
DuFrane is an independent, American-assembled watch microbrand founded by Steven Lee in 2016. The brand blends retro / vintage design cues (especially pilot, dive, field styles) with modern reliability and craftsmanship, aiming for watches that are stylish, durable, and full of character. The design philosophy is rooted in storytelling & place. Many of the model names and aesthetic touches are inspired by Austin, Texas (and its landmarks or history), and Lee’s own journey as a collector, designer, and former pilot. Each piece is hand-assembled in Austin (with the exception of some Swiss-made components, e.g. in the Littlefield GMT).
Target audience: watch enthusiasts and collectors who appreciate microbrands; people who like vintage and pilot/dive motifs but want modern build, honest pricing, artisanal touches, and a sense of place & personality in their timepiece. Also those who value that the founder is directly involved in assembly and service.
2016
Brand founded by Steven Lee in Austin, Texas; first model released (‘Bergstrom’), incorporating pilot-watch heritage and hand-wound Swiss movement. DuFrane is named after Lee's mother’s maiden name.
2017
Production pieces of the Bergstrom began shipping (initial sketches earlier, but production delayed until late 2016 / early 2017).
2021
Founder Steven Lee discussed the brand’s philosophy in interviews, emphasizing its Austin roots, design inspirations, personal assembly, customer experience.
2022
Release and reviews of new models such as the Travis, earning praise for design, case, lume, and finishing in microbrand watch circles.
2025
DuFrane continues a core product lineup including models like Waterloo, Mabry, Littlefield GMT etc., with both automatic and Swiss movement models; maintains assembly in Austin, and participates in watch shows such as Toronto Timepiece Show.

2016

H992
History
ARTICLES
H992 — Architecte Horloger — is an independent Swiss watch brand based in La Chaux-de-Fonds, a city situated at 992 metres above sea level in the Swiss Jura, and one of the most historically significant watchmaking centres in the world. The brand's name encodes its entire identity: H for Horology, H for Hour, H for Humanity, and 992 for the altitude of the city that made it.
The title Architecte Horloger — Watch Architect — defines the brand's approach: a discipline in which the logic of architecture and the precision of horology inform one another, and in which every design choice must justify itself structurally, functionally, and aesthetically.
La Chaux-de-Fonds, designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site alongside Le Locle, was built on a grid plan designed to optimise natural light for watchmakers working at their benches. It is a city shaped entirely by the demands of the craft. H992 carries that context as both foundation and philosophy.
HEADQUARTERS
La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland

RALF TECH
RALF TECH

History
ARTICLES
RALF TECH is a French independent watch manufacture founded in 1996 by Frank Huyghe — a professional diver, engineer, and watch collector whose obsession with technical precision led him to create timepieces that no mainstream brand would dare produce. Built on the philosophy of "Authenticity, Experience and Independence," RALF TECH designs professional-grade tool watches conceived for the most extreme environments on Earth: deep-sea diving, special forces operations, ocean racing, and even space exploration. Its tagline, "BE READY," is not a marketing slogan — it is an operational standard embedded in every component.
Production is fully integrated into the PEQUIGNET manufacture in Morteau, in the Doubs region of France, ensuring complete in-house control over quality and engineering. RALF TECH's collections — THE BEAST, WRX, WRV, WRB, and ACADÉMIE — each serve a distinct mission profile, from tactical military specifications to vintage-inspired elegance. THE BEAST, the brand's most ambitious creation, was certified to depths of -4,000 meters after over two years of intensive development, setting a new benchmark for dive watch engineering among independent manufactures.
With more than 100 special forces units equipped across 15+ countries, a world record autonomous dive to -330 meters in 2005, official timekeeping for the legendary Vendée Globe ocean race, and a collaboration with CNES — the French space agency — for astronaut-grade timepieces, RALF TECH has earned a rare status: a tool watch brand whose credentials are forged by real-world performance, not marketing campaigns.
1994
Founder Frank Huyghe, a professional diver and engineer, begins designing technical diving equipment and filing patents for underwater breathing systems — laying the groundwork for the RALF TECH watch philosophy.
1996
RALF TECH officially registered as a brand. The founding vision: create no-compromise technical tool watches for professionals operating in extreme environments, free from marketing constraints.
2003
Launch of the WR1, RALF TECH's first production watch — a professional dive timepiece engineered to military specifications and built to withstand the demands of real operational use.
2005
Frank Huyghe sets a world record for autonomous diving, descending to -330 meters — a testament to the brand's technical credibility and the real-world performance of its equipment.
2006
Development of the WR1-K for the French Navy's elite Commando Hubert unit, marking RALF TECH's entry into certified military supply and establishing its reputation among special forces worldwide.
2008
Introduction of the WRX, a tactical watch designed to meet the needs of special operations units. Its rugged construction and operational functionality made it a reference in military horology.
2011
WRX models delivered to special forces units across more than 15 countries, cementing RALF TECH's global standing as a trusted supplier for elite military and law enforcement professionals.
2012
Launch of the WRV, a vintage-inspired model drawing on 1970s tool watch aesthetics while maintaining RALF TECH's uncompromising technical standards — broadening the brand's appeal to collectors.
2013
RALF TECH becomes official timekeeper for the Vendée Globe, the legendary non-stop solo round-the-world ocean race — one of the most grueling events in competitive sailing.
2016
Introduction of the ACADÉMIE collection, offering a more classical and elegant expression of RALF TECH's engineering philosophy for professionals who require precision in formal contexts.
2019
Launch of the WRB, a versatile 39mm diver offering a more compact and daily-wear-oriented option, expanding RALF TECH's reach beyond professional users to a broader audience of enthusiasts.
2021
Production fully integrated into the PEQUIGNET manufacture in Morteau, France. THE BEAST certified to -3,000 meters — the result of over two years of intensive engineering and testing.
2022
Official launch of THE BEAST, RALF TECH's most ambitious watch, certified to -4,000 meters depth. The model set a new benchmark for independent dive watch engineering and became an instant collector reference.
2024
Partnership with CNES (the French National Centre for Space Studies) for the SPACE MILLENIUM collaboration, producing astronaut-grade timepieces — marking RALF TECH's expansion from the deep sea to outer space.
FOUNDED
1996
HEADQUARTERS
Paris, France

Rolex SA
ROLEX SA
History
ARTICLES
Rolex SA
is the private holding company that oversees
Rolex
and
Tudor
, two of the world's most respected watch brands. Founded by Hans Wilsdorf in London in 1905 and later relocated to Geneva, it operates as one of the most secretive and successful luxury companies in the world. Structured as a charitable foundation (the Hans Wilsdorf Foundation) since 1960, Rolex SA maintains complete independence, allowing for long-term strategic decisions without shareholder pressure. This unique structure has enabled consistent investment in manufacturing facilities, quality control, and vertical integration, and allowed
Rolex
to become among the most successful brands in history.
Brands summary:
Rolex
is the world's most recognized luxury watch brand, renowned for mechanical excellence, reliability, and value. Founded on principles of precision and durability,
Rolex
pioneered innovations including the first waterproof wristwatch (Oyster, 1926) and automatic winding (Perpetual, 1931). The brand offers a focused collection of iconic models including the Submariner, Datejust, GMT-Master, and Daytona, all characterized by incremental evolution rather than radical redesigns.
Tudor
was established by Wilsdorf in 1926 as a more accessible alternative to
Rolex
and continues to offer similar reliability and durability at lower price points. After decades of living in
Rolex
's shadow,
Tudor
underwent a dramatic revitalization beginning in 2010, developing a distinct identity focused on vintage-inspired designs with modern technical specifications. The Black Bay collection has spearheaded
Tudor
's renaissance, with the brand now recognized for offering exceptional value, in-house movements, and designs that balance heritage elements with contemporary styling.
FOUNDED
1905

Geneva, Switzerland

Arcanaut

History
ARTICLES
Arcanaut is an independent Scandinavian watch brand specializing in mechanical timepieces that fuse minimalist Nordic aesthetics with bold, experimental materials and craftsmanship. Known for its clean case silhouette and unconventional dial materials (such as Fordite and mussel-shell composites), the brand combines distinctive design elements (including a fully integrated square-crown case and in-house Scandinavian components) with Swiss-origin movements, catering to watch enthusiasts who appreciate innovative design rather than conventional luxury branding.
The design philosophy of Arcanaut centres on honesty in materials, ergonomic form, and an experimental mindset: their watches seek to build a “journey of discovery” for wearers, inviting them to engage in the story of design and production. Their key product lines include models such as the ARC I (a proof-of-concept debut in 2018) and the ARC II D’Arc Matter / Fordite series, which highlight unusual dials and limited production. Their target audience is the collector or design-minded purchaser who values craftsmanship, originality, and niche appeal over mass-market luxury labels.
Unique selling points include: a Scandinavian-manufactured case and components (in Denmark and Sweden) with Swiss-made movements, novel materials engineered by co-owner and “material alchemist” James Thompson (aka Black Badger) for dial construction, and a design language that avoids the typical dive/aviation clichés to deliver something visually distinct and concept-driven.
2015
Founding conversation between Anders Brandt and Simon Goldeman in Copenhagen that sparked the idea for the Arcanaut brand.
2018
Launch of the Swiss-made proof-of-concept model ARC I, limited series which sold out quickly.
2020
Materials specialist James ‘Black Badger’ Thompson joins Arcanaut as part-owner and Chief of Materials Development.
2023
Release of the ARC II Havender model featuring dial material made from locally-sourced mussel shells, showcasing Arcanaut’s experimental material approach.
FOUNDED
2015

Frederiksberg, Denmark

Patek Philippe
PATEKPHILIPPEGENEVA Official
History
ARTICLES
Patek Philippe & Co. is a Swiss watch brand that has been producing exceptional timepieces since its inception in 1839. This luxury brand is known for its precision, elegance, and durability, and has been a favorite of wealthy and sophisticated collectors for generations. Patek Philippe watches are not only renowned for their mechanical precision but also for their intricate designs that reflect years of craftsmanship and attention to detail. The company remains one of the last independent Geneva watch manufacturers and is widely recognized as one of the most prestigious watchmakers in the world.

1902

1925
The Calatrava design is introduced, becoming one of the brand's most iconic models
1933
The company produces the "Graves Supercomplication," a pocket watch with 24 complications that is considered the most complicated watch ever made at the time
1976
The company introduces the Nautilus, a sports watch designed by renowned watch designer Gérald Genta
1989
Patek Philippe celebrates its 150th anniversary and unveils the Calibre 89, a pocket watch with 33 complications, including a perpetual calendar, a moon phase, and a celestial chart
2001
The company introduces the Twenty-4 collection, its first line of ladies' watches
2019
The company unveils the Ref. 5212A-001, a weekly calendar watch with an innovative new function and design that pays homage to vintage timepieces.

Agelocer
History
ARTICLES
Agelocer is a forward-looking niche brand specializing in original mechanical watches. Registered in 2013, the company embraces independent movement design and production, fusing avant-garde innovation with traditional craftsmanship. Its ethos is encapsulated in the motto “Always Surprise,” marrying creative aesthetics and technical precision to cater to a modern audience seeking personality and mechanical artistry in timepieces.
Driven by high-quality in-house engineering, Agelocer delivers functional elegance at accessible price points. The brand develops its own movements—such as the Cal. A3350 square automatic, Cal. A4610 three-hand automatic, Cal. A9001 flying tourbillon, Cal. A4710 skeleton, and Cal. A4910 modern skeleton—boasting notable features like extended power reserves (up to ~80 hours), fine movement finishing, and complexity rarely seen at this level. These watches appeal to younger, style-conscious mechanical-watch enthusiasts who value innovation, detail, and value.
2013
Brand registration and establishment of Agelocer as a new-generation mechanical watch brand, emphasizing original design and independent movement R&D.
2013
Launch of the first self-developed movement, Cal. A3350 (square automatic with ~40 h power reserve).
2014
Introduction of Cal. A4610 calendar three-hand movement with ~80 h power reserve, marking a technical breakthrough.
2020–2021
Design acclaim achieved: Big Bang series wins 2020 London Design Award, 2021 Berlin Design Award, and 2021 Italian A’ Design Award.
2022
Agelocer’s Astronomer 6404A1, Schwarzwald 6001A1-R, and Sapphire Tourbillon 9201S11 win German Design Award for Excellent Product Design.
FOUNDED
2013

Mongkok, Hong Kong

WISE is a Thai watch brand specializing in high-quality timepieces that blend classic aesthetics with modern functionality. Established in 2008, the company has garnered attention for its meticulous craftsmanship and attention to detail. Each watch is designed in Thailand and hand-assembled by skilled watchmakers in Bangkok, ensuring a personalized touch in every piece.
WISE offers a diverse range of collections, including the ADAMASCUS series, known for its durable 904L stainless steel cases and limited-edition models featuring materials like meteorite and 24K gold-filled dials. The HITMAN collection features robust designs with options like full lume dials and FKM rubber straps, catering to both casual and professional tastes. Additionally, the ASURA series introduces Swiss automatic movements, appealing to enthusiasts seeking precision and reliability.

History
ARTICLES
Roamer of Switzerland is a heritage‑driven Swiss watchmaker specializing in elegantly designed, Swiss‑made wristwatches that emphasize quality, craftsmanship, and affordability. Known for integrating traditional watchmaking standards with timeless aesthetic cues, the brand delivers collections ranging from classic dress watches to sporty chronographs and diver models.
Rooted in a retro‑modern fusion, Roamer often blends tonneau‑shaped cases or open‑worked skeleton dials (“Competence Skeleton”) with modern materials like scratch‑resistant sapphire glass and in‑house updated movements such as the MST 2.0 and Mechano. Its pieces such as the R‑Line Classic showcase minimalist vintage charm with contemporary flair.
1888
Founded in Solothurn by Fritz Meyer; initially produced cylinder escapements before launching full watches and calibre No. 38
1905
Partnership Meyer & Stüdeli (MST) formed; development of in‑house movements and the MST brand
1955
Patent and launch of the Anfibio watertight case, giving rise to durable diver models
1972
Introduction of Roamer’s first quartz movement (microquartz)
2003
Christian Frommherz becomes CEO; steers brand back toward mechanical watches and affordable luxury
2013
Celebrated 125th anniversary with limited editions such as Stingray Chrono Diver
2024
Launch of the Mechano, the first watch powered by the updated MST 2.0 in‑house calibre
2025
Introduction of the Helios Power: the first Swiss‑made solar‑powered automatic chronograph from Roamer
FOUNDED
1888

Skagen
History
ARTICLES
Skagen watches are renowned for their minimalist and elegant designs, inspired by the clean lines and natural beauty of the Danish coastline. Founded in 1989, Skagen embodies the essence of "less is more," offering timepieces that blend form and function seamlessly. Known for their slim profiles and understated aesthetics, Skagen watches are crafted with high-quality materials such as stainless steel, leather, and titanium. They often feature simple yet sophisticated dials, often with a touch of Danish influence in their color palettes and design elements. Ideal for those who appreciate subtle elegance, Skagen watches provide timeless style and reliable performance.

KERBEDANZ
Loading videos...

History
ARTICLES
Kerbedanz is an independent Swiss watchmaking maison founded in 2011 in Neuchâtel, Switzerland, by two complementary visionaries: Kalust Zorik, a seasoned collector, and Aram Petrosyan, a designer. Together they built a brand on a single conviction: that a timepiece should not only measure seconds, but also express identity, legacy, and emotion.
The brand merges Swiss horological precision with design storytelling and symbolism. Every Kerbedanz creation is conceived, designed, and assembled in-house — from the first sketch to the final polish — under the creative direction of Chief Design Officer Aram Petrosyan. This complete in-house control ensures that every curve, engraving, and complication carries intentional symbolic resonance, giving life to watches that embody personal significance as much as mechanical mastery.
Kerbedanz describes its craft as a micromechanical show — a symphony of precision, movement, and light — achieved through a rigorous suite of artisanal techniques: micro-sculpture, engraving, enameling, gem-setting, and fine finishing, each coexisting with mechanical innovation. The brand calls this alliance of technical mastery and artistic expression its métier d'art, bridging centuries of Swiss horological tradition with an avant-garde spirit.
Through its Bespoke Program, Kerbedanz invites collectors to co-create timepieces that reflect their own singularity and values. For Kerbedanz, time is not simply counted — it is interpreted.
FOUNDED
2011

History

Clemence is a British microbrand in the watch industry, specialising in heritage-inspired tool and field watches that combine classic design cues (from the 1960s and ’70s) with modern robustness. Their design philosophy emphasises durability, longevity, and everyday adventure: watches that are built to endure rugged use (sea, summit, and urban exploration alike), yet have styling that nods to vintage timepieces. They’re assembled in Britain (with the help of international component specialists), and there is strong attention to craftsmanship, materials (sapphire crystals, solid steel bracelets, etc.), finishing, lume quality, and comfort.
Key products include the Photic Diver (a dive-style tool watch built to handle both underwater and daily wear, with vintage design references) and the Munro field watch (37.5 mm, slim but rugged, with British assembly, high-beat movement, etc.).

Invicta
Invicta Watch Official
Loading videos...

History
ARTICLES
Invicta is a Swiss-founded watchmaker (est. 1837) now headquartered in Hollywood, Florida. The brand specializes in bold, sporty timepieces and “affordable luxury” watches. Known for expertly built timepieces at accessible prices, Invicta offers large stainless-steel cases, eye-catching dials, and robust features (e.g. high water resistance and flame-fusion crystals) that combine classic horological elements (like Swiss movements and sapphire crystal) with modern technology. Its key collections – such as the diver-inspired Pro Diver, the premium Reserve series, and the racing-themed Invicta Racing models – embody a fusion of vintage-inspired design and contemporary innovation. The brand’s styling often “blends timeless elegance with modern design”, catering to fashion-conscious buyers and watch collectors who want statement-making watches on a budget.
Invicta’s design philosophy emphasizes meticulous craftsmanship and original concepts. The company prides itself on pushing “creative and technical revelations” in case construction and styling, which is evident in its over 30 distinct collections spanning diving, sport, and ornamental themes. Privately owned and still true to its founding Swiss ideology, Invicta offers features like automatic/chronograph movements and custom materials that appeal to both enthusiasts and general consumers. Its target audience ranges from younger, style-oriented buyers drawn to bold, oversized watches, to seasoned collectors seeking quality at value prices. In essence, Invicta’s unique selling points are its retro-modern aesthetic (rooted in Swiss heritage), its commitment to robust build quality (“meticulous art of case construction”), and its broad accessibility through affordable pricing.
1837
Founded by watchmaker Raphael Picard in La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland (Latin: Invicta means "invincible"), with a mission to offer high-quality mechanical timepieces at accessible prices.
1991
Acquired by investor Eyal Lalo; re-established in Hollywood, Florida, as Invicta Watch Company of America.  Lalo refocused the brand on modern automatic and quartz watches that blend Swiss movements and design with affordable pricing.
2000
Early 2000s – Introduced the Lupah and Pro Diver collections, featuring oversized cases (up to ~50 mm) and bold designs. These popular lines helped establish Invicta’s identity as a maker of dramatic, sporty watches.
2007
Launch of the Russian Diver series, based on a vintage Soviet naval prototype; originally a limited-edition release (2007) that became one of Invicta’s staple collections.
2016
Acquired historic Swiss brand Glycine Watch SA (founded 1914), expanding its portfolio while pledging to preserve Glycine’s identity.
2020
Partnered with NBA legend Shaquille O’Neal to create the Shaq Signature Series, a line of oversized specialty watches co-designed with the athlete.
FOUNDED
1837

Florida, United States

ZANNETTI
History
ARTICLES
Founded in Rome in 1982 by Riccardo Zannetti, ZANNETTI is an independent family-run Italian Haute Horlogerie manufacture rooted in a multi-generational passion for fine craftsmanship. The family story begins with Carmine Zannetti, a master goldsmith and watch enthusiast, followed by Mario Zannetti, a design professor and international artist whose love of geometric lines and mechanical complexity still shapes the brand today. Riccardo inherited this cultural legacy and united it with the skills of a great watchmaker and an artist — designing every watch exclusively by hand, from pencil sketch to finished piece.
Each Zannetti timepiece is unique, numbered, and registered. Riccardo does not merely customise aesthetics or mechanics in isolation — he reinterprets a watch in its entirety, creating pieces that are impossible to copy and perfectly finished in every technical detail. The brand is one of the few artisan workshops still carrying on a tradition that began 500 years ago, spreading Italian creativity across Europe, Asia, the Middle East, and the USA.
The flagship boutique is located in the historic Via di Monte d'Oro 23 in Rome, the city that has always been the soul of the brand.

1989

2000
The Squelette — first Zannetti skeleton watch — introduced, featuring the first blue PVD treatment on a skeleton movement worldwide
2020
Creation of the original Zannetti movement, entirely Made in Italy
2022
40th Anniversary of the brand; release of the Regent Anniversary collection
2023
New Regent collection launched with the original Zannetti mechanical movement, Made in Italy

History

Grand Seiko is a prestigious Japanese watch brand renowned for its precision, craftsmanship, and elegant design. Established in 1960 as a subsidiary of Seiko, Grand Seiko aims to produce high-end timepieces that rival the finest Swiss watches. Each watch is meticulously crafted, combining traditional techniques with cutting-edge technology. Notable for their Spring Drive and Hi-Beat movements, Grand Seiko watches are celebrated for their exceptional accuracy and distinctive aesthetic, embodying the Japanese principles of simplicity, beauty, and excellence. The brand has earned a dedicated following among watch enthusiasts for its commitment to quality and innovation.

HYT
HYT WATCHES Official
History
ARTICLES
HYT is a Swiss luxury watchmaker renowned for its groundbreaking integration of fluid mechanics with traditional horology. Established in 2012, HYT introduced a unique approach to timekeeping by utilizing fluids to display time, drawing inspiration from ancient water clocks known as clepsydras. This innovative design features two immiscible liquids—one colored and one transparent—that move within a glass capillary tube to indicate hours, driven by bellows that expand and contract in harmony with the mechanical movement. This seamless fusion of fluid dynamics and mechanical engineering exemplifies HYT’s commitment to challenging conventional watchmaking norms and redefining the art of time measurement.

History
ARTICLES
Atlantic Watches is a distinguished Swiss watchmaker renowned for its rich heritage and commitment to quality craftsmanship. Established in 1888 by Eduard Kummer in Bettlach, near Grenchen, Switzerland, the company began as EKB (Eduard Kummer Bettlach), producing components for esteemed brands like Longines and Certina. In 1932, Atlantic transitioned to producing complete timepieces under its own name, introducing one of the first waterproof chronographs. A significant innovation came in 1960 with the development of the "Speed Switch," a mechanism that enabled the date to change 52 times faster than previous systems, setting a milestone in watchmaking history.
Today, Atlantic Watches continues to embody Swiss precision and timeless design. Their collections encompass a range of timepieces, including automatic, mechanical, and quartz watches, featuring renowned movements such as ETA and Ronda. The brand is celebrated for its nautical-inspired designs, exemplified by models like the Worldmaster and Seacrest lines. Atlantic watches are characterized by their use of high-quality materials, including sapphire crystals and stainless steel cases, and are designed to offer both elegance and durability. Catering to a diverse audience, Atlantic appeals to watch enthusiasts seeking reliable, stylish, and accessible Swiss-made timepieces.
1888
Founded by Eduard Kummer in Bettlach, Switzerland, as EKB, producing components for brands like Longines and Certina.
1932
Began producing complete timepieces under the Atlantic name, introducing one of the first waterproof chronographs.
1952
Renamed to Atlantic Watch Ltd, consolidating its identity in the watchmaking industry.
1960
Developed the 'Speed Switch' mechanism, allowing the date to change 52 times faster, a significant innovation in watchmaking.
2008
Moved headquarters from Grenchen to Lengnau, Switzerland.
2025
Continues to produce a diverse range of Swiss-made timepieces, including limited editions and models featuring mechanical manufacture calibres.
FOUNDED
1888

Eterna

History
ARTICLES
The origins of the Eterna watch brand may be traced all the way back to 1856 in Switzerland. The business has established a reputation for producing clocks of exceptional quality and accuracy, as well as for its pioneering attitude, having introduced a number of significant watchmaking advancements throughout the course of its history. The designs of Eterna watches are often recognized for their timelessness and refinement, as well as their use of luxurious components and cutting-edge technology in their construction. The business has a long history of making watches for several industries, including aviation, diving, and sports, and it is well-known for the durability and dependability of its timepieces.
1856
The company is founded by Josef Girard and Urs Schild in Grenchen, Switzerland.
1870
Eterna becomes one of the first Swiss watch brands to produce watch movements with ball bearings, leading to improved accuracy and durability.
1914
Eterna develops the first wristwatch with an alarm function, which becomes a popular design feature in subsequent years.
1932
Eterna introduces the "Eterna-matic" automatic movement, which features a rotor system that winds the watch using the motion of the wearer's wrist.
1848
Eterna introduces the "KonTiki" watch, named after the famous Kon-Tiki raft expedition, which features a robust and water-resistant design for use in sports and diving.
1978
Eterna introduces the "Golden Heart" watch, which features a transparent caseback that showcases the watch's movement.
1995
Eterna introduces the "Spherodrive" movement, which replaces traditional gears with ball bearings, leading to improved accuracy and durability.
2011
Eterna is acquired by Chinese watch company Citychamp Watch & Jewellery Group.

History
ARTICLES
Von Doren is a Norwegian heritage-inspired watch brand specializing in elegant mechanical and quartz wristwatches that reflect both Norwegian storytelling and high-quality Swiss watchmaking. The brand was founded in 2016 by Øyvind VonDoren Asbjørnsen, a filmmaker whose passion for timepieces began when he inherited his great-grandfather’s Swiss watch — an experience that sparked a lifelong fascination with horology and design. Von Doren’s timepieces are designed in Norway and built with Swiss movements, combining clean Scandinavian aesthetics with reliable technical foundations.
The brand’s design philosophy emphasizes timelessness, understated sophistication, and narrative depth, often drawing inspiration from Norwegian nature, culture, film, and industrial heritage. Collections like URÆD GMT — a modern sports watch honoring a real Norwegian Atlantic crossing — and playful lines such as Runde Candy, inspired by the puffins on the island of Runde, reflect this blend of story and style. Von Doren also creates watches inspired by iconic cultural motifs like Il Tempo Gigante from Norway’s beloved film Flåklypa Grand Prix.
2016
Von Doren Watch Company AS was founded by Norwegian filmmaker Øyvind VonDoren Asbjørnsen, inspired by his great-grandfather’s Swiss watch and a lifelong passion for watches.
2017

2020

2023

2025
Expansion of narrative-driven timepieces including the *URÆD GMT*, a dual-time model paying tribute to historic Norwegian adventures, reflecting continued evolution of Von Doren’s design language.

The brand’s philosophy centers on intentional design and personal connection: its founder spent over a decade in the watch industry before launching Mezei, motivated by a desire to create timepieces that feel unique, grounded, and built to last.

History
ARTICLES
Abingdon Co. is a female-owned luxury watch brand specializing in high-performance, stylish timepieces designed for adventurous women in male-dominated fields like aviation, scuba diving, motocross, and tactical professions. Known for purpose-built craftsmanship, the brand merges Swiss/Japanese movements, durable materials (e.g., surgical-grade stainless steel, sapphire crystals), and gender-inclusive functionality with elegant, customizable designs. Its watches, such as the aviation-focused Amelia (featuring an E6B flight calculator) and the Marina Dive Watch (200m water resistance, titanium case), cater to women who demand both utility and sophistication in their gear 311. The brand’s design philosophy centers on empowerment and community, exemplified by its Abingdon Crew, a global network of women pilots, divers, and professionals. Unique selling points include customizable straps, collaborations with industry pioneers (e.g., Women Airforce Service Pilots via the WASP Watch), and philanthropic initiatives like the Abingdon Foundation, which funds scholarships for women in STEM and aviation 5811. Targeting active women who "do more", Abingdon balances affordability with luxury, positioning itself as a symbol of resilience and female leadership in horology 911.
2006
Founded by Abingdon Mullin, a pilot and adventurer, during a roundtable discussion with women pilots seeking functional, feminine timepieces. The company ethos emerged from this dialogue, focusing on women's needs in male-dominated fields.
2007
Launched inaugural collections: the Jackie (aviator chronograph) and Amelia (dual-time zone with E6B flight calculator), establishing the brand as the first female-centric aviation watchmaker.
2012
Forged a pivotal partnership with Alibaba.com after founder Abingdon Mullin's testimonial at CES, leading to global promotional support and expanded market reach.
2016
Introduced the It's About Time scholarship program at the Women in Aviation conference, supporting aspiring female pilots and divers through mentorship and financial aid.
2017
Established the Abingdon Foundation to empower women in STEM and outdoor industries via sponsorships, community outreach, and educational initiatives.
2022
Expanded retail presence through Macy's and military exchanges. Launched the Nadia Dive Watch, tested by Women Divers Hall of Fame inductees, featuring a bi-directional compass and screw-down crown for enhanced water resistance.
2023
Introduced the Try-Me Box program for pool professionals, offering discounted valves/actuators, and the Golden Ticket initiative for exclusive facility tours.
2025
Received acclaim for the Marina Dive Watch, praised for its titanium case and 200m water resistance. Released the limited-edition WASP Watch, honoring WWII Women Airforce Service Pilots with historical design elements.
FOUNDED
2006

Nevada, United States

NOCTUA
NOCTUA WATCH CORP. Official
History
ARTICLES
Inspired by Athene Noctua, the owl of wisdom and strategy, Noctua Watch Corp. blends timeless craftsmanship with precision engineering. Every watch is meticulously handmade in Canada by a single artisan, using premium imported components parts. Produced in small batches to uphold the highest quality, each timepiece undergoes rigorous in-house testing before it reaches your hands. Noctua is more than a watch—it’s a statement of intelligence, perseverance, and enduring craftsmanship. Designed for those who appreciate thoughtful design and meticulous execution, Noctua watches are built to accompany you on your journey, just as wisdom guided the greatest adventurers of history.
